 761:         self._last_error = None
 762:         self._last_response_result = None
 763:         self.logger.info(
 764:             f"[Request:{request_id_api}] APIClient calling handler {type(self.client_handler).__name__}.get_response session={session_id_ctx} model={getattr(self.model_config, 'model', None)} stream={use_streaming}"
 765:         )
 766:         try:
 767:             safe_msgs_for_log = []
 768:             for msg in prepared_messages:
 769:                 role = msg.get("role", "unknown")
 770:                 content_log = "[omitted]"
 771:                 if isinstance(msg.get("content"), str):
 772:                     content_log = msg["content"][:100] + (
 773:                         "..." if len(msg["content"]) > 100 else ""
 774:                     )
 775:                 elif isinstance(msg.get("content"), list):
 776:                     content_log = (
 777:                         f"[{len(msg['content'])} parts: "
 778:                         + ", ".join([p.get("type", "unknown") for p in msg["content"]])
 779:                         + "]"
 780:                     )
 781:                 safe_msgs_for_log.append({"role": role, "content": content_log})
 782:             self.logger.debug(
 783:                 f"[Request:{request_id_api}] Messages passed to handler (summary): {safe_msgs_for_log}"
 784:             )
 785: 
 786:             # <<<--- Add Token Count Logging ---<<<
 787:             try:
 788:                 final_token_count = self.count_tokens(prepared_messages)
 789:                 estimated_input_tokens = final_token_count
 790:                 self.logger.info(
 791:                     f"[Request:{request_id_api}] Estimated token count for prepared messages: {final_token_count}"
 792:                 )
 793:                 if final_token_count > (
 794:                     self.max_history_tokens * 0.95
 795:                 ):  # Warn if close to limit
 796:                     self.logger.warning(
 797:                         f"[Request:{request_id_api}] Prepared message token count ({final_token_count}) is close to limit ({self.max_history_tokens})."
 798:                     )
 799:             except Exception as count_err:
 800:                 self.logger.warning(
 801:                     f"[Request:{request_id_api}] Failed to count tokens for prepared messages: {count_err}"
 802:                 )
 803:             # >>> --- End Token Count Logging --- >>>
 804: 
 805:         except Exception as log_err:
 806:             self.logger.warning(
 807:                 f"[Request:{request_id_api}] Error creating safe log/counting tokens for prepared_messages: {log_err}"
 808:             )
 809:         # >>>------------------------------------------- >>>
 810: 
 811:         try:
 812:             # --- Ideal Flow (Enforce Interface - See Step 2) ---
 813:             if not hasattr(self.client_handler, "get_response"):
 814:                 # This should ideally not happen if Step 2 is done.
 815:                 self.logger.error(
 816:                     f"CRITICAL: Client handler {type(self.client_handler).__name__} missing required 'get_response' method!"
 817:                 )
 818:                 response_text = (
 819:                     f"[Error: Handler {type(self.client_handler).__name__} "
 820:                     "interface mismatch]"
 821:                 )
 822:                 self._last_response_result = self._build_response_result(
 823:                     text=response_text,
 824:                     error=build_llm_error(
 825:                         message=response_text,
 826:                         provider=getattr(self.model_config, "provider", None),
 827:                         model=getattr(self.model_config, "model", None),
 828:                         category=ErrorCategory.RUNTIME,
 829:                         retryable=False,
 830:                     ),
 831:                 )
 832:                 return response_text
 833: 
 834:             # Normalize callback to async (chunk, message_type) signature
 835:             effective_callback = (
 836:                 adapt_stream_callback(stream_callback) if use_streaming else None
 837:             )
 838:             self.logger.debug(
 839:                 f"[APIClient:{request_id_api}] Calling {type(self.client_handler).__name__}.get_response. Streaming: {use_streaming}. Callback: {effective_callback is not None}"
 840:             )
 841: 
 842:             self.logger.info(
 843:                 f"[APIClient:{request_id_api}] PRE-CALL TO HANDLER: use_streaming={use_streaming}, effective_callback is {effective_callback}"
 844:             )
 845: 
 846:             effective_max_output_tokens = (
 847:                 max_output_tokens or self.model_config.max_output_tokens
 848:             )
 849:             raw_context_limit = getattr(
 850:                 self.model_config, "max_context_window_tokens", None
 851:             )
 852:             try:
 853:                 context_limit_int = (
 854:                     int(raw_context_limit)
 855:                     if raw_context_limit is not None and int(raw_context_limit) > 0
 856:                     else None
 857:                 )
 858:             except Exception:
 859:                 context_limit_int = None
 860:             if (
 861:                 context_limit_int is not None
 862:                 and estimated_input_tokens is not None
 863:                 and estimated_input_tokens > 0
 864:             ):
 865:                 available_output_tokens = max(
 866:                     context_limit_int - estimated_input_tokens - 512,
 867:                     1,
 868:                 )
 869:                 if (
 870:                     effective_max_output_tokens is None
 871:                     or effective_max_output_tokens > available_output_tokens
 872:                 ):
 873:                     self.logger.warning(
 874:                         "[APIClient:%s] Clamping max_output_tokens from %s to %s "
 875:                         "based on context_limit=%s estimated_input_tokens=%s",
 876:                         request_id_api,
 877:                         effective_max_output_tokens,
 878:                         available_output_tokens,
 879:                         context_limit_int,
 880:                         estimated_input_tokens,
 881:                     )
 882:                     effective_max_output_tokens = available_output_tokens
 883: 
 884:             response_text = await self.client_handler.get_response(
 885:                 messages=prepared_messages,
 886:                 max_output_tokens=effective_max_output_tokens,
 887:                 temperature=temperature
 888:                 if temperature is not None
 889:                 else self.model_config.temperature,
 890:                 stream=use_streaming,
--- 604-670
 604:     async def prepare_request(
 605:         self,
 606:         messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],
 607:         max_output_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
 608:         temperature: Optional[float] = None,
 609:         stream: Optional[bool] = None,
 610:         **kwargs: Any,
 611:     ) -> LLMPreparedRequest:
 612:         """Prepare the provider-native request without sending network traffic."""
 613: 
 614:         if not self.client_handler:
 615:             raise RuntimeError("APIClient handler not initialized.")
 616: 
 617:         legacy_max_tokens = kwargs.pop("max_tokens", None)
 618:         if max_output_tokens is None and legacy_max_tokens is not None:
 619:             max_output_tokens = legacy_max_tokens
 620: 
 621:         use_streaming = (
 622:             stream if stream is not None else self.model_config.streaming_enabled
 623:         )
 624:         prepared_messages = self._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt(messages)
 625:         preparer = getattr(self.client_handler, "prepare_request", None)
 626:         if not callable(preparer):
 627:             raise RuntimeError(
 628:                 f"Handler {type(self.client_handler).__name__} does not support "
 629:                 "request preparation"
 630:             )
 631: 
 632:         prepared = preparer(
 633:             messages=prepared_messages,
 634:             max_output_tokens=max_output_tokens
 635:             or self.model_config.max_output_tokens,
 636:             temperature=temperature
 637:             if temperature is not None
 638:             else self.model_config.temperature,
 639:             stream=bool(use_streaming),
 640:             **kwargs,
 641:         )
 642:         if asyncio.iscoroutine(prepared):
 643:             prepared = await prepared
 644:         if not isinstance(prepared, LLMPreparedRequest):
 645:             raise RuntimeError(
 646:                 f"Handler {type(self.client_handler).__name__} returned invalid "
 647:                 "prepared request"
 648:             )
 649:         return prepared
 650: 
 651:     def get_reasoning_debug_snapshot(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 652:         """Return handler-provided reasoning debug details when available."""
 653: 
 654:         handler = getattr(self, "client_handler", None)
 655:         getter = getattr(handler, "get_reasoning_debug_snapshot", None)
 656:         if callable(getter):
 657:             try:
 658:                 snapshot = getter()
 659:             except Exception:
 660:                 self.logger.debug(
 661:                     "Failed to get handler reasoning debug snapshot", exc_info=True
 662:                 )
 663:                 return {}
 664:             if isinstance(snapshot, dict):
 665:                 return dict(snapshot)
 666:         return {}
 667: 
 668:     def _format_user_error_message(self, *, error: LLMError, diag_id: str) -> str:
 669:         category = error.category.value
 670:         provider_detail = ""

**Planning a test case**

I'm focusing on creating a test in the `tests/llm/test_prepared_request_contract.py` file using a stub handler. I need to inspect the relevant parts, so I'll probably use `read_file` to deal with some lines. I noticed that some outputs are clipped, which might mean I should use `sed` to limit the length. Specifically, I'll look to read sections around the `get_response` lines from 840 to 885 for more context.

I found the real remaining bug: Penguin is inventing an output cap from remaining context. Patching that now.

   882	                    effective_max_output_tokens = available_output_tokens

   885	                messages=prepared_messages,

   887	                temperature=temperature

   889	                else self.model_config.temperature,
   890	                stream=use_streaming,

120	    def get_instance(
   121	        cls, config: Optional[ConnectionPoolConfig] = None
   122	    ) -> "ConnectionPoolManager":
   123	        """Get the singleton instance (sync accessor).
   124	
   125	        Args:
   126	            config: Optional configuration for new instances
   127	
   128	        Returns:
   129	            The global ConnectionPoolManager instance
   130	        """
   131	        if cls._instance is None:
   132	            cls._instance = cls(config or ConnectionPoolConfig())
   133	        return cls._instance
   134	
   135	    @classmethod
   136	    async def get_instance_async(
   137	        cls, config: Optional[ConnectionPoolConfig] = None
   138	    ) -> "ConnectionPoolManager":
   139	        """Get the singleton instance (async accessor with lock).
   140	
   141	        Args:
   142	            config: Optional configuration for new instances
   143	
   144	        Returns:
   145	            The global ConnectionPoolManager instance
   146	        """
   147	        if cls._instance is None:
   148	            async with cls._lock:
   149	                if cls._instance is None:
   150	                    cls._instance = cls(config or ConnectionPoolConfig())
   151	        return cls._instance
   152	
   153	    def __init__(self, config: Optional[ConnectionPoolConfig] = None):
   154	        """Initialize the connection pool manager.
   155	
   156	        Args:
   157	            config: Configuration for pool limits and timeouts
   158	        """
   159	        self._config = config or ConnectionPoolConfig()
   160	        self._clients: Dict[str, httpx.AsyncClient] = {}
   161	        self._client_locks: Dict[str, asyncio.Lock] = {}
   162	
   163	    async def get_client(self, base_url: str) -> httpx.AsyncClient:
   164	        """Get or create a pooled client for the given base URL.
   165	
   166	        Args:
   167	            base_url: The base URL for the API (e.g., "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1")
   168	
   169	        Returns:
   170	            Shared AsyncClient with connection pooling
   171	        """
   172	        if base_url not in self._clients:
   173	            # Create lock for this base_url if needed
   174	            if base_url not in self._client_locks:
   175	                self._client_locks[base_url] = asyncio.Lock()
   176	
   177	            async with self._client_locks[base_url]:
   178	                # Double-check after acquiring lock
   179	                if base_url not in self._clients:
   180	                    self._clients[base_url] = httpx.AsyncClient(
   181	                        limits=self._config.to_limits(),
   182	                        timeout=self._config.to_timeout(),
   183	                    )
   184	                    logger.info(f"Created connection pool for {base_url}")
   185	
   186	        return self._clients[base_url]
   187	
   188	    @asynccontextmanager
   189	    async def client_context(self, base_url: str):
   190	        """Context manager for using a pooled client.
   191	
   192	        Unlike the per-request pattern, this reuses connections:
   193	
   194	        Old (inefficient):
   195	            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
   196	                response = await client.post(...)
   197	
   198	        New (connection pooling):
   199	            pool = ConnectionPoolManager.get_instance()
   200	            async with pool.client_context(base_url) as client:
   201	                response = await client.post(...)
   202	
   203	        Args:
   204	            base_url: The base URL for the API
   205	
   206	        Yields:
   207	            Shared AsyncClient instance
   208	        """
   209	        client = await self.get_client(base_url)
   210	        try:
   211	            yield client
   212	        except httpx.PoolTimeout:
   213	            logger.warning(
   214	                f"Connection pool timeout for {base_url}, request may be delayed"
   215	            )
   216	            raise
   217	
   218	    async def close_all(self) -> None:
   219	        """Close all pooled clients. Call during shutdown."""
   220	        for base_url, client in list(self._clients.items()):
```

270	class APIClient:
   271	    """
   272	    A client for interacting with various AI model APIs, routing requests
   273	    to either a native adapter or the LiteLLM gateway based on configuration.
   274	
   275	    Attributes:
   276	        model_config (ModelConfig): Configuration for the AI model.
   277	        system_prompt (str): The system prompt to be sent with each request.

   279	        logger (logging.Logger): Logger for this class.
   280	    """
   281	
   282	    def __init__(
   283	        self,
   284	        model_config: ModelConfig,
   285	        *,
   286	        base_url: Optional[str] = None,
   287	        extra_headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
   288	    ):
   289	        """
   290	        Initialize the APIClient.
   291	
   292	        Args:
   293	            model_config (ModelConfig): Configuration for the AI model, including
   294	                                        the 'client_preference'.
   295	        """
   296	        self.model_config = model_config
   297	        self._canonicalize_native_model_id()
   298	        self.system_prompt = None
   299	        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   300	        self.client_handler = None  # Will be set based on preference
   301	        self._last_error: Optional[LLMError] = None
   302	        self._last_response_result: Optional[LLMCallResult] = None
   303	        self._base_url = base_url
   304	        self._extra_headers = dict(extra_headers or {})

   306	            native_adapter_factory=get_adapter,
   307	            litellm_gateway_loader=load_litellm_gateway_class,
   308	        )

   311	            f"Initializing APIClient for model: {model_config.model}, "
   312	            f"Provider: {model_config.provider}, "
   313	            f"Preference: {model_config.client_preference}"
   314	        )
   315	
   316	        # --- Instantiate the correct handler via the shared registry ---
   317	        try:
   318	            self.client_handler = self.provider_registry.create_handler(
   319	                model_config,
   320	                base_url=self._base_url,
   321	                extra_headers=self._extra_headers,

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_delegates_prepared_request_to_handler(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_ACCOUNT_ID", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "penguin.llm.adapters.openai.get_provider_credential",
        lambda _provider_id: None,
    )
    handler = build_openai_handler(
        provider="openai",
        stream_events=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=OPENAI_USAGE,
    )
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = handler.model_config
    client.system_prompt = "Injected system prompt."
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    prepared = await client.prepare_request(
        [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
        max_output_tokens=111,
        stream=False,
    )

    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_responses"
    assert "Injected system prompt." in str(prepared.body["input"])
    assert prepared.body["max_output_tokens"] == 111


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_does_not_synthesize_max_tokens_from_context_window() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = -1

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = build_openrouter_handler().model_config
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = build_openrouter_handler().model_config
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46500
```

)

            self.logger.info(
                f"[APIClient:{request_id_api}] PRE-CALL TO HANDLER: use_streaming={use_streaming}, effective_callback is {effective_callback}"
            )

            effective_max_output_tokens = (
                max_output_tokens or self.model_config.max_output_tokens
            )
            raw_context_limit = getattr(
                self.model_config, "max_context_window_tokens", None
            )
            try:
                context_limit_int = (
                    int(raw_context_limit)
                    if raw_context_limit is not None and int(raw_context_limit) > 0
                    else None
                )
            except Exception:
                context_limit_int = None
            if (
                context_limit_int is not None
                and estimated_input_tokens is not None
                and estimated_input_tokens > 0
                and effective_max_output_tokens is not None
            ):
                available_output_tokens = max(
                    context_limit_int - estimated_input_tokens - 512,
                    1,
                )
                if effective_max_output_tokens > available_output_tokens:
                    self.logger.warning(
                        "[APIClient:%s] Clamping max_output_tokens from %s to %s "
                        "based on context_limit=%s estimated_input_tokens=%s",
                        request_id_api,
                        effective_max_output_tokens,
                        available_output_tokens,
                        context_limit_int,
                        estimated_input_tokens,
                    )
                    effective_max_output_tokens = available_output_tokens

            response_text = await self.client_handler.get_response(
                messages=prepared_messages,
                max_output_tokens=effective_max_output_tokens,

                if temperature is not None
                else self.model_config.temperature,

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_anthropic_prepares_messages_request() -> None:
    handler = build_anthropic_handler(
        stream_chunks=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=ANTHROPIC_USAGE,
    )
    tools = [
        {
            "name": "read_file",
            "description": "Read a file",
            "input_schema": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"}},
            },
        }
    ]

    prepared = await handler.prepare_request(
        _messages(),
        max_output_tokens=321,
        temperature=0.2,
        stream=True,
        tools=tools,
    )

    assert prepared.provider == "anthropic"
    assert prepared.protocol == "anthropic_messages"
    assert prepared.route == "anthropic.messages"
    assert prepared.transport == "sdk_stream"
    assert prepared.body["system"] == "System rules."
    assert prepared.body["max_tokens"] == 321
    assert prepared.body["temperature"] == 0.2
    assert prepared.body["messages"][0]["role"] == "user"
    assert prepared.body["tools"] == tools
    assert prepared.capabilities is not None
    assert prepared.capabilities.native_tools is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_openrouter_prepares_chat_completions_request(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    handler = build_openrouter_handler(
        monkeypatch,
        stream_chunks=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=OPENROUTER_USAGE,
    )
    handler.extra_headers["X-Test"] = "prepared"

    prepared = await handler.prepare_request(
        _messages(),
        max_output_tokens=456,
        temperature=0.3,
        stream=True,
        tools=_tool_schema(),
    )

    assert prepared.provider == "openrouter"
    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_chat_completions"
    assert prepared.route == "openrouter.chat_completions"
    assert prepared.transport == "sdk_stream"
    assert prepared.headers["X-Test"] == "prepared"
    assert prepared.body["model"] == "openai/gpt-4.1-mini"
    assert prepared.body["max_tokens"] == 456
    assert prepared.body["temperature"] == 0.3
    assert prepared.body["stream"] is True
    assert prepared.body["stream_options"] == {"include_usage": True}
    assert "extra_headers" not in prepared.body
    assert prepared.body["tools"][0]["type"] == "function"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_delegates_prepared_request_to_handler(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_ACCOUNT_ID", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "penguin.llm.adapters.openai.get_provider_credential",
        lambda _provider_id: None,
    )
    handler = build_openai_handler(
        provider="openai",
        stream_events=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=OPENAI_USAGE,
    )
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = handler.model_config
    client.system_prompt = "Injected system prompt."
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    prepared = await client.prepare_request(
        [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
        max_output_tokens=111,
        stream=False,
    )

    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_responses"
    assert "Injected system prompt." in str(prepared.body["input"])
    assert prepared.body["max_output_tokens"] == 111


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_does_not_synthesize_max_tokens_from_context_window() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = -1

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = build_openrouter_handler().model_config
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None

    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN", raising=False)

        "penguin.llm.adapters.openai.get_provider_credential",
        lambda _provider_id: None,
    )

        provider="openai",
        stream_events=[],

        [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
        max_output_tokens=111,
        stream=False,
    )

    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_responses"
    assert "Injected system prompt." in str(prepared.body["input"])
    assert prepared.body["max_output_tokens"] == 111


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_does_not_synthesize_max_tokens_from_context_window() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = -1

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = build_openrouter_handler().model_config
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = build_openrouter_handler().model_config
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46500

)

            self.logger.info(
                f"[APIClient:{request_id_api}] PRE-CALL TO HANDLER: use_streaming={use_streaming}, effective_callback is {effective_callback}"
            )

            effective_max_output_tokens = (
                max_output_tokens or self.model_config.max_output_tokens
            )
            raw_context_limit = getattr(
                self.model_config, "max_context_window_tokens", None
            )
            try:
                context_limit_int = (
                    int(raw_context_limit)
                    if raw_context_limit is not None and int(raw_context_limit) > 0
                    else None
                )
            except Exception:
                context_limit_int = None
            if (
                context_limit_int is not None
                and estimated_input_tokens is not None
                and estimated_input_tokens > 0
                and effective_max_output_tokens is not None
            ):
                available_output_tokens = max(
                    context_limit_int - estimated_input_tokens - 512,
                    1,
                )
                if effective_max_output_tokens > available_output_tokens:
                    self.logger.warning(
                        "[APIClient:%s] Clamping max_output_tokens from %s to %s "
                        "based on context_limit=%s estimated_input_tokens=%s",
                        request_id_api,
                        effective_max_output_tokens,
                        available_output_tokens,
                        context_limit_int,
                        estimated_input_tokens,
                    )
                    effective_max_output_tokens = available_output_tokens

            response_text = await self.client_handler.get_response(
                messages=prepared_messages,
                max_output_tokens=effective_max_output_tokens,
                temperature=temperature
                if temperature is not None
                else self.model_config.temperature,

+    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
+    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
+    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421
+
+    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)
+
+    assert result == "ok"
+    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
+    class _Handler:
+        model_config = None
+        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None
+
+        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
+            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")
+
+        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
+            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
+            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
+            return "ok"
+
+    handler = _Handler()
+    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
+    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
+        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
+        provider="openrouter",
+        client_preference="openrouter",
+    )
+    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
+    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
+    client.system_prompt = None
+    client.client_handler = handler
+    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+    client._last_error = None
+    client._last_response_result = None
+    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
+    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
+    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
+    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
+    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000
+
+    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)
+
+    assert result == "ok"
+    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46500

from __future__ import annotations

import logging

import pytest

from penguin.llm.api_client import APIClient
from penguin.llm.model_config import ModelConfig
from penguin.llm.contracts import (
    LLMPreparedRequest,
    LLMProviderCapabilities,
    stable_payload_hash,
)

from .provider_contract_fixtures import (
    ANTHROPIC_USAGE,
    OPENAI_USAGE,
    OPENROUTER_USAGE,
    build_anthropic_handler,
    build_openai_handler,
    build_openrouter_handler,
)


def _messages() -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    return [
        {"role": "system", "content": "System rules."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
    ]


def _tool_schema() -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    return [
        {
            "type": "function",
            "function": {
                "name": "read_file",
                "description": "Read a file",
                "parameters": {
                    "type": "object",
        monkeypatch,
        stream_chunks=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=OPENROUTER_USAGE,
    )
    handler.extra_headers["X-Test"] = "prepared"

    prepared = await handler.prepare_request(
        _messages(),
        max_output_tokens=456,
        temperature=0.3,
        stream=True,
        tools=_tool_schema(),
    )

    assert prepared.provider == "openrouter"
    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_chat_completions"
    assert prepared.route == "openrouter.chat_completions"
    assert prepared.transport == "sdk_stream"
    assert prepared.headers["X-Test"] == "prepared"
    assert prepared.body["model"] == "openai/gpt-4.1-mini"
    assert prepared.body["max_tokens"] == 456
    assert prepared.body["temperature"] == 0.3
    assert prepared.body["stream"] is True
    assert prepared.body["stream_options"] == {"include_usage": True}
    assert "extra_headers" not in prepared.body
    assert prepared.body["tools"][0]["type"] == "function"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_delegates_prepared_request_to_handler(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_ACCOUNT_ID", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "penguin.llm.adapters.openai.get_provider_credential",
        lambda _provider_id: None,
    )
    handler = build_openai_handler(
        provider="openai",
        stream_events=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=OPENAI_USAGE,
    )
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = handler.model_config
    client.system_prompt = "Injected system prompt."
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    prepared = await client.prepare_request(
        [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
        max_output_tokens=111,
        stream=False,
    )

    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_responses"
    assert "Injected system prompt." in str(prepared.body["input"])
    assert prepared.body["max_output_tokens"] == 111


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_does_not_synthesize_max_tokens_from_context_window() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = -1

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
        provider="openrouter",
        client_preference="openrouter",
    )
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
        provider="openrouter",
        client_preference="openrouter",
    )
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46500

async def test_api_client_delegates_prepared_request_to_handler(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_ACCOUNT_ID", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "penguin.llm.adapters.openai.get_provider_credential",
        lambda _provider_id: None,
    )
    handler = build_openai_handler(
        provider="openai",
        stream_events=[],
        final_text="answer",
        usage=OPENAI_USAGE,
    )
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = handler.model_config
    client.system_prompt = "Injected system prompt."
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    prepared = await client.prepare_request(
        [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
        max_output_tokens=111,
        stream=False,
    )

    assert prepared.protocol == "openai_responses"
    assert "Injected system prompt." in str(prepared.body["input"])
    assert prepared.body["max_output_tokens"] == 111


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_does_not_synthesize_max_tokens_from_context_window() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = -1

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
        provider="openrouter",
        client_preference="openrouter",
    )
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
        provider="openrouter",
        client_preference="openrouter",
    )
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46500

async def test_api_client_does_not_synthesize_max_tokens_from_context_window() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = -1

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
        provider="openrouter",
        client_preference="openrouter",
    )
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
    class _Handler:
        model_config = None
        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None

        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")

        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
            return "ok"

    handler = _Handler()
    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
        provider="openrouter",
        client_preference="openrouter",
    )
    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
    client.system_prompt = None
    client.client_handler = handler
    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    client._last_error = None
    client._last_response_result = None
    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000

    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)

    assert result == "ok"
    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46500
```

_Thinking:_

**Evaluating test failures**

I need to identify why the second test failed. It could be that modifications to client.model_config affected previous build_openrouter_handler() replacements. I think we might have replaced all occurrences of "client.model_config = build_openrouter_handler().model_config," but perhaps we only needed to do that for two instances. I'll inspect the test lines between 258 and 360 completely using read_file and then run the failed single test to check the output. The tests might also be hanging.

The “don’t synthesize” regression passes; the explicit-clamp regression is failing. I’m pulling the exact assertion now.

+            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")
+
+        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
+            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
+            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
+            return "ok"
+
+    handler = _Handler()
+    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
+    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
+        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
+        provider="openrouter",
+        client_preference="openrouter",
+    )
+    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
+    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = None
+    client.system_prompt = None
+    client.client_handler = handler
+    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+    client._last_error = None
+    client._last_response_result = None
+    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
+    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
+    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
+    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
+    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 46421
+
+    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)
+
+    assert result == "ok"
+    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens is None
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_api_client_clamps_explicit_max_tokens_to_available_context() -> None:
+    class _Handler:
+        model_config = None
+        received_max_output_tokens: int | None = None
+
+        def get_capabilities(self) -> LLMProviderCapabilities:
+            return LLMProviderCapabilities(provider="openrouter")
+
+        async def get_response(self, **kwargs: object) -> str:
+            value = kwargs.get("max_output_tokens")
+            self.received_max_output_tokens = value if isinstance(value, int) else None
+            return "ok"
+
+    handler = _Handler()
+    client = APIClient.__new__(APIClient)
+    client.model_config = ModelConfig(
+        model="moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
+        provider="openrouter",
+        client_preference="openrouter",
+    )
+    client.model_config.max_context_window_tokens = 272000
+    client.model_config.max_output_tokens = 100000
+    client.system_prompt = None
+    client.client_handler = handler
+    client.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+    client._last_error = None
+    client._last_response_result = None
+    client._clear_handler_error = lambda: None
+    client._get_handler_error = lambda: None
+    client._build_response_result = lambda **kwargs: None
+    client._prepare_messages_with_system_prompt = lambda messages: messages
+    client.count_tokens = lambda _messages: 225000
+
+    result = await client.get_response(_messages(), stream=False)
+
+    assert result == "ok"
+    assert handler.received_max_output_tokens == 46488
```

_Thinking:_



You’re right to suspect config, but the **actual remaining bug was in runtime request budgeting**.

**What Happened**
- Your log says output requested: `225067`.
- Local/root config had `context_window: 272000`.
- Penguin computed: `272000 - ~46421 input - 512 buffer = 225067`.
- That means Penguin was **inventing `max_tokens` from remaining context** when no explicit output cap existed. Bad. Context budget ≠ output budget.

**Why PR #56 Wasn’t Enough**
- PR #56 fixed model metadata/config resolution.
- But `penguin/llm/api_client.py` still did this later:
  - If `effective_max_output_tokens is None`, set it to remaining context.
- So even with `max_output_tokens: null`, the request still sent a huge `max_tokens`.

**Fix Made**
- New branch: `fix/openrouter-dont-synthesize-output-cap`
- Commit: `97d2524d3 bug: do not synthesize output cap from context`
- Changed `APIClient` to:
  - Leave `max_output_tokens` unset when no explicit cap exists.
  - Still clamp explicit caps if they exceed remaining context.
- Validated:
  - `pytest tests/llm/test_prepared_request_contract.py -q`
  - Broader focused suite: 51 tests passing
  - `git diff --check`

Next move: open a follow-up PR or push this into the existing flow.

---

_Thinking:_



PR created: https://github.com/Maximooch/penguin/pull/58

**What I Think From The Logs**
- The original `max_tokens ~= 225k` issue is fixed by PR #58. That was Penguin manufacturing output cap from remaining context.
- Slowness is real but not one thing:
  - First calls: `31s`, `19s`, `19s` with `cache_read=0` → cold provider/cache path.
  - Later calls: `4.8s–10s` with `cache_read≈50k` → cache starts helping.
  - One call: `161s`, `reasoning_tokens=2618`, response only `103 chars` → likely bad provider routing/model behavior, not local cache.
- Empty responses are mostly **tool-only turns**:
  - `response_len=0 pending_tool_call=True`
  - Then Penguin persists `message_len=27` placeholder.
  - That’s not necessarily model failure, but the UX/logging makes it look broken. We should distinguish “tool call only” from “empty model response.”
- OpenRouter config likely needs provider preferences:
  - Kimi via OpenRouter is sometimes slow/erratic.
  - We should set sane provider routing, probably prefer lower-latency/stable providers and avoid known slow ones where OpenRouter supports it.
- Also: context is huge. You’re sending ~38k–61k input tokens repeatedly. Caching helps, but this is still expensive latency-wise. The prompt/context loader is doing too much by default.

**Recommended Next PRs**
1. Add OpenRouter provider routing config support + diagnostics log selected provider if available.
2. Fix streaming/tool-only logging so it says `tool_only=True`, not “empty.”
3. Audit context autoload: 24k tokens of project docs on a fresh task is excessive. That’s a tax, not intelligence.

1141:     def format_token_usage_simple(self) -> str:
1142:         """Simple token usage formatting without dependencies"""
1143:         usage = self.get_token_usage()
1144:         total = usage.get("total", 0)
1145:         available = usage.get("available", 0)
1146:         max_context_window_tokens = usage.get("max", 0)
1147: 
1148:         # Calculate percentage of total budget used
1149:         percentage = (
1150:             (total / max_context_window_tokens * 100)
1151:             if max_context_window_tokens
1152:             else 0
1153:         )
1154: 
1155:         output = [
1156:             f"Token Usage: {total:,}/{max_context_window_tokens:,} ({percentage:.1f}%)",
1157:             f"Available: {available:,} tokens",
1158:             "\nBy Category:",
1159:         ]
1160: 
1161:         # Add category breakdowns
1162:         for category in MessageCategory:
1163:             category_tokens = usage.get(str(category), 0)
1164:             budget = self._budgets.get(category)
1165:             if budget:
1166:                 category_pct = (
1167:                     (category_tokens / budget.max_category_tokens * 100)
1168:                     if budget.max_category_tokens
1169:                     else 0
1170:                 )
1171:                 output.append(
1172:                     f"  {category.name}: {category_tokens:,}/{budget.max_category_tokens:,} ({category_pct:.1f}%)"
1173:                 )
1174: 
1175:         return "\n".join(output)
1176: 
1177:     def get_usage(self, category: MessageCategory) -> int:
1178:         """Get current token usage for a specific category."""
1179:         budget = self._budgets.get(category)
1180:         return budget.current_tokens if budget else 0
1181: 
1182:     # =================================================================
1183:     # Phase 2 Extensions: Dynamic Reallocation and Context Contributors
1184:     # =================================================================
1185: 
1186:     def get_available_tokens(self, category: MessageCategory) -> int:
1187:         """Get available tokens for a category (max - current)"""
1188:         budget = self._budgets.get(category)
1189:         if not budget:
1190:             return 0
1191:         return max(0, budget.max_category_tokens - budget.current_tokens)
1192: 
1193:     def borrow_tokens(
1194:         self, from_category: MessageCategory, to_category: MessageCategory, amount: int
1195:     ) -> bool:
1196:         """
1197:         Borrow unused tokens from one category to another.
1198: 
1199:         Args:
1200:             from_category: Category to borrow tokens from
1201:             to_category: Category to lend tokens to
1202:             amount: Number of tokens to borrow
1203: 
1204:         Returns:
1205:             True if borrowing was successful
1206:         """
1207:         from_budget = self._budgets.get(from_category)
1208:         to_budget = self._budgets.get(to_category)
1209: 
1210:         if not from_budget or not to_budget:
1211:             return False
1212: 
1213:         # Check if from_category has enough unused tokens
1214:         available_from = from_budget.max_category_tokens - from_budget.current_tokens
1215:         if available_from < amount:
1216:             return False
1217: 
1218:         # Perform the borrowing
1219:         from_budget.max_category_tokens -= amount
1220:         to_budget.max_category_tokens += amount
1221: 
1222:         logger.debug(
1223:             f"Borrowed {amount} tokens from {from_category.name} to {to_category.name}"
1224:         )
1225:         return True
1226: 
1227:     def auto_rebalance_budgets(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
1228:         """
1229:         Automatically rebalance budgets based on current usage patterns.
1230:         Working files can borrow from DIALOG when needed.
1231: 
1232:         Returns:
1233:             Dict showing token movements
1234:         """
1235:         movements = {}
1236: 
1237:         # Priority for borrowing: CONTEXT (working files) can borrow from DIALOG
1238:         context_budget = self._budgets.get(MessageCategory.CONTEXT)
1239:         dialog_budget = self._budgets.get(MessageCategory.DIALOG)
1240: 
1241:         if context_budget and dialog_budget:
1242:             # If CONTEXT is over budget and DIALOG has unused tokens
1243:             context_over = (
1244:                 context_budget.current_tokens - context_budget.max_category_tokens
1245:             )
1246:             dialog_available = (
1247:                 dialog_budget.max_category_tokens - dialog_budget.current_tokens
1248:             )
1249: 
1250:             if context_over > 0 and dialog_available > 0:
1251:                 # Borrow up to what's needed or available
1252:                 borrow_amount = min(
1253:                     context_over, dialog_available, dialog_available // 2
1254:                 )  # Max 50% of available
1255: 
1256:                 if self.borrow_tokens(
1257:                     MessageCategory.DIALOG, MessageCategory.CONTEXT, borrow_amount
1258:                 ):
1259:                     movements[f"DIALOG -> CONTEXT"] = borrow_amount
1260: 
1261:         return movements
1262: 
1263:     def load_project_instructions(
1264:         self, workspace_root: str = "."
1265:     ) -> Tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
1266:         """
1267:         Load project instruction files (PENGUIN.md, AGENTS.md, README.md) with simple autoloading.
1268:         Simplified version without complex contributor system.
1269: 
1270:         Args:
1271:             workspace_root: Root directory of the workspace
1272: 
1273:         Returns:
1274:             Tuple of (project_content, debug_info)
1275:         """
1276:         from pathlib import Path
1277: 
1278:         workspace = Path(workspace_root).resolve()
1279:         content_parts = []
1280:         loaded_files = []
1281: 
1282:         # Priority order: PENGUIN.md > AGENTS.md > README.md
1283:         doc_files = [
1284:             ("PENGUIN.md", "Project Instructions", 2400),  # 600 tokens worth
1285:             ("AGENTS.md", "Agent Specifications", 2000),  # 500 tokens worth
1286:             ("README.md", "Project Overview", 1200),  # 300 tokens worth
1287:         ]
1288: 
1289:         for filename, section_title, max_chars in doc_files:
1290:             doc_path = workspace / filename
1291:             if doc_path.exists():
1292:                 try:
1293:                     with open(doc_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
1294:                         content = f.read()
1295:                         # Limit content to prevent context overflow
1296:                         if len(content) > max_chars:
1297:                             content = content[:max_chars] + "\n... (truncated)"
1298:                         content_parts.append(
1299:                             f"## {section_title} ({filename})\n{content}"
1300:                         )
1301:                         loaded_files.append(filename)
1302: 
1303:                         # If we found PENGUIN.md or AGENTS.md, don't fall back to README
1304:                         if filename in ["PENGUIN.md", "AGENTS.md"]:
1305:                             break
1306: 
1307:                 except Exception as e:
1308:                     logger.warning(f"Failed to read {filename}: {e}")
1309: 
1310:         assembled_content = "\n\n".join(content_parts) if content_parts else ""
1311:         token_count = self.token_counter(assembled_content) if assembled_content else 0
1312: 
1313:         # Compute truncation flag correctly by re-evaluating based on the
1314:         # stored parts lengths versus their caps. Track truncation during load
1315:         # to avoid referencing out-of-scope variables.
1316:         truncated = False
1317:         try:
1318:             # If any chosen file's content was longer than its max_chars, we appended
1319:             # the marker; use that as a reliable signal.
1320:             truncated = any(
1321:                 part.endswith("... (truncated)") or "(truncated)" in part
1322:                 for part in content_parts
1323:             )
1324:         except Exception:
1325:             truncated = False
1326: 
1327:         debug_info = {
1328:             "loaded_files": loaded_files,
1329:             "total_tokens": token_count,
1330:             "files_checked": [f[0] for f in doc_files],
1331:             "truncated": truncated,
1332:         }
1333: 
1334:         return assembled_content, debug_info
1335: 
1336:     def get_allocation_report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1337:         """
1338:         Generate a detailed allocation report for debugging.
1339:         Shows how tokens are allocated across categories and contributors.
1340:         """
1341:         report = {
1342:             "total_budget": self.max_context_window_tokens,
1343:             "categories": {},
1344:             "utilization": {},
1345:         }
1346: 
1347:         total_used = 0
1348:         for category, budget in self._budgets.items():
1349:             category_info = {
1350:                 "min_tokens": budget.min_tokens,
1351:                 "max_context_window_tokens": budget.max_category_tokens,
1352:                 "current_tokens": budget.current_tokens,
1353:                 "available": budget.max_category_tokens - budget.current_tokens,
1354:                 "utilization_pct": (
1355:                     (budget.current_tokens / budget.max_category_tokens * 100)
1356:                     if budget.max_category_tokens
1357:                     else 0
1358:                 ),
1359:             }
1360:             report["categories"][category.name] = category_info
1361:             total_used += budget.current_tokens
1362: 
1363:         report["utilization"]["total_used"] = total_used
1364:         report["utilization"]["total_available"] = (
1365:             self.max_context_window_tokens - total_used
1366:         )
1367:         report["utilization"]["overall_pct"] = (
1368:             (total_used / self.max_context_window_tokens * 100)
1369:             if self.max_context_window_tokens
1370:             else 0
1371:         )
1372: 
1373:         return report

   1: # Context Window Manager
   2: 
   3: The `ContextWindowManager` v0.4.0 handles advanced token budgeting and content trimming for conversation context with multimodal content handling and dynamic budget allocation.
   4: 
   5: ## Overview
   6: 
   7: The ContextWindowManager is responsible for:
   8: 
   9: 1. **Advanced Token Budgeting**: Dynamic allocation based on content type and priority
  10: 2. **Image Handling**: Intelligent trimming of multimodal content to preserve tokens
  11: 3. **Category-Based Management**: Fine-grained control over different message types
  12: 4. **Real-time Usage Tracking**: Live monitoring of token consumption
  13: 5. **Smart Trimming Strategies**: Context-aware content removal with recency preservation
  14: 6. **Multi-Provider Token Counting**: Automatic fallback tokenization strategies
  15: 7. **Rich Reporting**: Human-readable and programmatic token usage statistics
  16: 
  17: ## Message Categories and Priorities
  18: 
  19: Messages are categorized into five priority levels for intelligent trimming:
  20: 
  21: 1. **SYSTEM** (highest priority): System instructions and prompts - **never trimmed** (10% budget)
  22: 2. **CONTEXT**: Important reference information, documentation, and context files (30% budget)
  23: 3. **DIALOG**: Main conversation between user and assistant (40% budget)
  24: 4. **SYSTEM_OUTPUT**: Tool outputs, code execution results, and action responses (20% budget)
  25: 5. **ERROR**: Error messages and debugging information (fallback 5% budget)
  26: 
  27: ### Priority System
  28: 
  29: - **SYSTEM messages**: Guaranteed preservation with strict 10% minimum allocation
  30: - **Chronological trimming**: Within each category, oldest messages are trimmed first to preserve recency
  31: - **Dynamic budget management**: Automatic budget redistribution when categories are under-utilized
  32: - **Image-aware processing**: Special handling for multimodal content with token-intensive elements
  33: - **Fallback categories**: Automatic handling of new message types with default budgets
  34: 
  35: ```mermaid
  36: pie title Token Budget Allocation v0.4.0
  37:     "SYSTEM" : 10
  38:     "CONTEXT" : 30
  39:     "DIALOG" : 40
  40:     "SYSTEM_OUTPUT" : 20
  41:     "ERROR (fallback)" : 5
  42: ```
  43: 
  44: ## Trimming Strategy
  45: 
  46: ```mermaid
  47: flowchart TD
  48:     Start([Start]) --> AnalyzeSession[Analyze Session & Count Tokens]
  49:     AnalyzeSession --> ImageCheck{Contains Multiple Images?}
  50: 
  51:     ImageCheck -- Yes --> HandleImages[Replace Old Images with Placeholders]
  52:     HandleImages --> AnalyzeSession
  53: 
  54:     ImageCheck -- No --> CheckBudget{Total Over Budget?}
  55: 
  56:     CheckBudget -- Yes --> PriorityTrim[Trim by Priority Order]
  57:     PriorityTrim --> TrimLowest[Trim ERROR/SYSTEM_OUTPUT First]
  58:     TrimLowest --> CheckBudget1{Still Over Budget?}
  59: 
  60:     CheckBudget1 -- Yes --> TrimDialog[Trim DIALOG Messages]
  61:     TrimDialog --> CheckBudget2{Still Over Budget?}
  62: 
  63:     CheckBudget2 -- Yes --> TrimContext[Trim CONTEXT Messages]
  64:     TrimContext --> PreserveSystem[Preserve SYSTEM Messages]
  65:     PreserveSystem --> Reassemble[Reassemble Session]
  66: 
  67:     CheckBudget2 -- No --> PreserveSystem
  68:     CheckBudget1 -- No --> PreserveSystem
  69: 
  70:     CheckBudget -- No --> CategoryCheck{Any Category Over Individual Budget?}
  71: 
  72:     CategoryCheck -- Yes --> TrimSpecific[Trim Specific Category]
  73:     TrimSpecific --> Reassemble
  74: 
  75:     CategoryCheck -- No --> Return[Return Original Session]
  76: 
  77:     Reassemble --> Return
  78: 
  79:     style HandleImages fill:#bbf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
  80:     style TrimLowest fill:#f99,stroke:#333
  81:     style TrimDialog fill:#f96,stroke:#333
  82:     style TrimContext fill:#ff6,stroke:#333
  83:     style PreserveSystem fill:#6f6,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
  84: ```
  85: 
  86: ## Special Content Handling

  88: ```mermaid

 117: 
 118: ## Initialization
 119: 
 120: ```python
 121: def __init__(
 122:     self,
 123:     model_config=None,
 124:     token_counter: Optional[Callable[[Any], int]] = None,
 125:     api_client=None,
 126:     config_obj: Optional[Any] = None,
 127: )
 128: ```
 129: 
 130: ### Smart Configuration Resolution
 131: 
 132: The ContextWindowManager automatically resolves configuration from multiple sources in priority order:
 133: 
 134: 1. **Model Configuration**: `model_config.max_context_window_tokens` (if provided)
 135: 2. **Live Config Object**: `config_obj.model_config.max_history_tokens` (preferred)
 136: 3. **Global Config**: `config.yml` model settings
 137: 4. **Fallback**: Default 150,000 tokens
 138: 
 139: ### Intelligent Token Counter Selection
 140: 
 141: The system automatically selects the best available token counter:
 142: 
 143: 1. **API Client Counter**: `api_client.count_tokens()` (provider-specific)
 144: 2. **Diagnostics Counter**: `diagnostics.count_tokens()` (tiktoken-based)
 145: 3. **Fallback Counter**: Rough estimation (4 chars per token)
 146: 
 147: ### Features
 148: 
 149: - **Dynamic Max Tokens**: Adapts to model capabilities automatically
 150: - **Multi-source Configuration**: Supports live config updates
 151: - **Provider-aware Tokenization**: Uses provider-specific token counting when available
 152: - **Robust Fallbacks**: Graceful degradation when components are unavailable
 153: 
 154: ### Clamp Notices (Sub-Agents)
 155: 
 156: When a sub-agent is created with an isolated context window and a `shared_context_window_max_tokens` value, the child's `ContextWindowManager.max_context_window_tokens` is set to the lower of the parent’s max and the provided clamp. A system note with metadata `type=cw_clamp_notice` is recorded on both the parent and child conversations to make this visible in transcripts and dashboards. The metadata includes:
 157: 
 158: - `sub_agent`: child agent id
 159: - `child_max`: effective child max tokens
 160: - `parent_max`: parent’s max tokens at time of spawn
 161: - `clamped`: true if the child was reduced relative to parent
 162:

 270: ## Advanced Trimming Strategy
 271: 
 272: The trimming strategy v0.4.0 follows a sophisticated multi-pass approach:
 273: 
 274: ### Phase 1: Image Optimization
 275: 1. **Multi-image Detection**: Identify sessions with multiple images
 276: 2. **Recency Preservation**: Keep the most recent image intact
 277: 3. **Token-Efficient Replacement**: Replace older images with placeholders
 278: 4. **Metadata Preservation**: Store original image references for context
 279: 
 280: ### Phase 2: Budget Analysis
 281: 1. **Total Budget Check**: Compare session tokens against model limits
 282: 2. **Category Budget Analysis**: Check individual category limits
 283: 3. **Priority Queue Creation**: Order categories by trim priority
 284: 
 285: ### Phase 3: Intelligent Trimming
 286: 1. **Chronological Trimming**: Remove oldest messages first within each category
 287: 2. **Minimum Budget Enforcement**: Respect minimum token allocations
 288: 3. **SYSTEM Protection**: Never trim SYSTEM messages
 289: 4. **Iterative Reduction**: Continue trimming until budget is met
 290: 
 291: ### Phase 4: Session Reconstruction
 292: 1. **Order Preservation**: Maintain original message ordering
 293: 2. **Category Integrity**: Keep messages in their logical categories
 294: 3. **Metadata Updates**: Update session statistics and metadata
 295: 
 296: ## Image Handling
 297: 
 298: The system includes specialized handling for multimodal content:
 299: 
 300: ### Image Token Estimation
 301: - **Claude Models**: ~4,000 tokens per image (higher for safety)
 302: - **Vision Models**: Provider-specific token calculations
 303: - **Fallback Estimation**: Character-based approximation
 304: 
 305: ### Smart Image Trimming
 306: ```python
 307: def _handle_image_trimming(self, session: Session) -> Session:
 308:     """
 309:     Replace all but the most recent image with placeholders.
 310:     Preserves conversation context while saving significant tokens.
 311:     """
 312: ```
 313: 
 314: ### Placeholder Generation
 315: - **Context Preservation**: Maintains conversation flow
 316: - **Reference Tracking**: Stores original image metadata
 317: - **User-Friendly Messages**: Clear indication of image removal
 318: 
 319: ## Token Counter Fallback Chain
 320: 
 321: The system implements a robust fallback strategy for token counting:
 322: 
 323: 1. **Provider-Specific Counter**: Uses native model tokenizer (optimal accuracy)
 324: 2. **LiteLLM Generic Counter**: Fallback to model-specific tiktoken
 325: 3. **Diagnostics Counter**: Uses tiktoken with model mapping
 326: 4. **Character Estimation**: Rough fallback (4 chars = 1 token)
 327: 
 328: This ensures accurate token counting across all scenarios while maintaining performance.
 329: 
 330: ## Advanced Usage Examples
 331: 
 332: ### Basic Setup with Configuration
 333: 
 334: ```python
 335: from penguin.system.context_window import ContextWindowManager
 336: from penguin.llm.model_config import ModelConfig
 337: 
 338: # Initialize with configuration
 339: context_window = ContextWindowManager(
 340:     model_config=model_config,
 341:     api_client=api_client,
 342:     config_obj=live_config  # Supports live config updates
 343: )
 344: 
 345: # Automatic configuration resolution from multiple sources
 346: print(f"Max tokens: {context_window.max_context_window_tokens}")
 347: print(f"Available tokens: {context_window.available_tokens}")
 348: ```
 349: 
 350: ### Comprehensive Session Analysis
 351: 
 352: ```python
 353: # Analyze session with multimodal content detection
 354: stats = context_window.analyze_session(session)
 355: print(f"Total tokens: {stats['total_tokens']}")
 356: print(f"Images detected: {stats['image_count']}")
 357: print(f"Tokens by category: {stats['per_category']}")
 358: print(f"Over budget: {stats['over_budget']}")
 359: 
 360: # Detailed breakdown
 361: for category, tokens in stats['per_category'].items():
 362:     percentage = (tokens / stats['total_tokens']) * 100 if stats['total_tokens'] > 0 else 0
 363:     print(f"  {category.name}: {tokens} tokens ({percentage:.1f}%)")
 364: ```
 365: 
 366: ### Intelligent Session Processing
 367: 
 368: ```python
 369: # Process session with automatic trimming and optimization
 370: original_token_count = context_window.analyze_session(session)['total_tokens']
 371: trimmed_session = context_window.process_session(session)
 372: final_token_count = context_window.analyze_session(trimmed_session)['total_tokens']
 373: 
 374: print(f"Original tokens: {original_token_count}")
 375: print(f"Final tokens: {final_token_count}")
 376: print(f"Reduction: {original_token_count - final_token_count} tokens")
 377: 
 378: # Session integrity preserved
 379: print(f"Messages removed: {len(session.messages) - len(trimmed_session.messages)}")
 380: print(f"SYSTEM messages preserved: {len([m for m in trimmed_session.messages if m.category.name == 'SYSTEM'])}")
 381: ```
 382: 
 383: ### Real-time Token Monitoring
 384: 
 385: ```python
 386: # Monitor token usage in real-time
 387: usage = context_window.get_token_usage()
 388: print(context_window.format_token_usage())
 389: 
 390: # Rich formatting for CLI
 391: if rich_available:
 392:     print(context_window.format_token_usage_rich())
 393: 
 394: # Programmatic access
 395: for category in MessageCategory:
 396:     budget = context_window.get_budget(category)
 397:     usage = context_window.get_usage(category)
 398:     percentage = (usage / budget.max_category_tokens) * 100 if budget.max_category_tokens > 0 else 0
 399:     print(f"{category.name}: {usage}/{budget.max_category_tokens} ({percentage:.1f}%)")
 400: ```
 401: 
 402: ### Advanced Category Management
 403: 
 404: ```python
 405: # Update usage tracking manually
 406: context_window.update_usage(MessageCategory.CONTEXT, 5000)
 407: 
 408: # Check if any category is over budget
 409: for category in MessageCategory:
 410:     if context_window.is_over_budget(category):
 411:         print(f"Warning: {category.name} is over budget!")
 412: 
 413: # Reset usage tracking
 414: context_window.reset_usage()  # Reset all
 415: context_window.reset_usage(MessageCategory.DIALOG)  # Reset specific category
 416: ```
 417: 
 418: ## Token Budgeting System
 419: 
 420: The dynamic token budget allocation system v0.4.0 includes:
 421: 
 422: ### Intelligent Budget Distribution
 423: - **SYSTEM**: 10% (guaranteed preservation, strict minimum enforcement)
 424: - **CONTEXT**: 30% (documentation and reference materials)
 425: - **DIALOG**: 40% (main conversation, flexible allocation)
 426: - **SYSTEM_OUTPUT**: 20% (tool results and action outputs)
 427: - **ERROR**: 5% (fallback for error messages and debugging)
 428: 
 429: ### Dynamic Budget Management
 430: - **Automatic Redistribution**: Unused budget from one category can benefit others
 431: - **Minimum Guarantees**: Each category has a minimum token allocation
 432: - **Maximum Limits**: Prevents any category from consuming excessive tokens
 433: - **Runtime Adjustments**: Budgets can be modified during session processing
 434: 
 435: ### Token Efficiency Features
 436: - **Image Optimization**: Intelligent replacement of redundant images
 437: - **Smart Trimming**: Chronological removal preserving conversation coherence
 438: - **Category-Aware Processing**: Different strategies for different content types
 439: - **Metadata Preservation**: Maintains conversation context during optimization
 440: 
 441: This system ensures optimal token utilization while preserving conversation quality and system functionality.
