   1: # It could use some improvements, but it's a good start.
   2: 
   3: """
   4: Context window management for managing token budgets and content trimming.
   5: 
   6: This module provides tools to:
   7: 1. Track token usage across different message categories
   8: 2. Manage trimming strategies based on content types
   9: 3. Handle special content types like images
  10: 4. Ensure context windows don't exceed model limits
  11: """
  12: 
  13: # Future ideas:
  14: # - Make the budgets dynamic based on the content of the messages. Maybe?
  15: # - Don't hardcode the categories, make them configurable. Which means here it deals with the budgets of categories based on the priority they're infered from the config.
  16: 
  17: # for multi/sub-agents, each will have their own context window manager that sits in that agent's shared context window manager
  18: # they will also be able to inherit some settings from the parent context window manager
  19: # these can't be too big, but they can't be too small either.
  20: # this is one of the reasons i wanted to split out conversation modules (where the conversation can be processed like a queue)
  21: # if its in that, then its a different conversation as far as the context window manager is concerned
  22: # in theory, this could extend to tool calls themselves, that could then have their own context window manager
  23: # yeah, an agent calling a tool that calls a tool that calls a tool etc
  24: 
  25: """
  26: Possibly in terms of Cognitive Architectures, Context Windows could be representated as States/Objects 
  27: that can be accessed by more than just one Agent. 
  28: 
  29: Meaning you could have sub-agents that represent functions similar to what a Human brain could do 
  30: in terms of short/long term memory.
  31: """
  32: 
  33: # Also worth having a features / roadmap / competitors analysis.md files, for Penguin and what others are doing.
  34: 
  35: # TODO:
  36: # - Add a function to get the total token usage for a session
  37: # - Add a function to get the token usage for a specific category
  38: # - Add a function to get the token usage as a percentage of the total budget
  39: # - Add a function to get the token usage for a specific message
  40: # - Add a function to get the token usage for a specific message category
  41: # - Add a function to get the token usage for a specific message role
  42: 
  43: # TODO:
  44: # - Don't hardcode the categories, make them configurable. Which means here it deals with the budgets of categories based on the priority they're infered from the config.
  45: # - Make the budgets dynamic based on the content of the messages. Maybe?
  46: 
  47: 
  48: import logging
  49: import warnings
  50: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  51: from datetime import datetime
  52: from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Union, Any, Callable, Tuple
  53: 
  54: from penguin.system.state import Message, MessageCategory, Session
  55: 
  56: from penguin.constants import CONTEXT_UNCATEGORIZED_BUDGET_FRACTION
  57: 
  58: from penguin.constants import get_default_context_window_emergency_fallback_tokens
  59: 
  60: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
  61: 
  62: 
  63: def _category_name(category: Any) -> str:
  64:     """Return a stable category name for diagnostics."""
  65: 
  66:     return getattr(category, "name", str(category or "UNKNOWN"))
  67: 
  68: 
  69: def _content_chars(value: Any) -> int:
  70:     """Return content size in characters for context diagnostics."""
  71: 
  72:     if isinstance(value, str):
  73:         return len(value)
  74:     return len(str(value))
  75: 
  76: 
  77: def _message_preview(value: Any, limit: int = 120) -> str:
  78:     """Return a compact single-line message preview for logs."""
  79: 
  80:     text = str(value or "").replace("\n", "\\n")
  81:     if len(text) <= limit:
  82:         return text
  83:     return text[: max(limit - 3, 0)] + "..."
  84: 
  85: 
  86: @dataclass
  87: class TokenBudget:
  88:     """Token budget for a message category"""
  89: 
  90:     # Minimum tokens guaranteed for this category
  91:     min_tokens: int
  92:     # Maximum tokens this category can consume
  93:     max_category_tokens: int  # Max tokens this category can consume
  94:     # Current tokens used by this category
  95:     current_tokens: int = 0
  96: 
  97:     def __post_init__(self):
  98:         # Ensure min_tokens <= max_category_tokens
  99:         if self.min_tokens > self.max_category_tokens:
 100:             logger.warning(
 101:                 f"min_tokens ({self.min_tokens}) > max_category_tokens ({self.max_category_tokens}). "
 102:                 "Setting min_tokens = max_category_tokens"
 103:             )
 104:             self.min_tokens = self.max_category_tokens
 105: 
 106: 
 107: @dataclass
 108: class TruncationEvent:
 109:     """Record of a context window truncation event"""
 110: 
 111:     category: MessageCategory
 112:     messages_removed: int
 113:     tokens_freed: int
 114:     timestamp: str
 115:     total_messages_before: int
 116:     total_messages_after: int
 117: 
 118: 
 119: class TruncationTracker:
 120:     """Tracks context window truncation events for UI display"""
 121: 
 122:     def __init__(self):
 123:         self.events: List[TruncationEvent] = []
 124:         self.per_category: Dict[MessageCategory, List[TruncationEvent]] = {
 125:             cat: [] for cat in MessageCategory
 126:         }
 127:         self.session_total_truncations = 0
 128:         self.session_total_messages_removed = 0
 129:         self.session_total_tokens_freed = 0
 130: 
 131:     def record_truncation(
 132:         self,
 133:         category: MessageCategory,
 134:         messages_removed: int,
 135:         tokens_freed: int,
 136:         total_messages_before: int,
 137:         total_messages_after: int,
 138:     ) -> TruncationEvent:
 139:         """Record a truncation event and return it"""
 140:         event = TruncationEvent(
 141:             category=category,
 142:             messages_removed=messages_removed,
 143:             tokens_freed=tokens_freed,
 144:             timestamp=datetime.now().isoformat(),
 145:             total_messages_before=total_messages_before,
 146:             total_messages_after=total_messages_after,
 147:         )
 148: 
 149:         self.events.append(event)
 150:         self.per_category[category].append(event)
 151:         self.session_total_truncations += 1
 152:         self.session_total_messages_removed += messages_removed
 153:         self.session_total_tokens_freed += tokens_freed
 154: 
 155:         logger.info(
 156:             f"Truncation recorded: {category.name} - "
 157:             f"{messages_removed} messages, {tokens_freed} tokens freed"
 158:         )
 159: 
 160:         return event
 161: 
 162:     def get_category_truncations(self, category: MessageCategory) -> int:
 163:         """Get total number of messages truncated for a category"""
 164:         return sum(e.messages_removed for e in self.per_category[category])
 165: 
 166:     def get_recent_events(self, limit: int = 10) -> List[TruncationEvent]:
 167:         """Get most recent truncation events"""
 168:         return self.events[-limit:] if self.events else []
 169: 
 170:     def reset(self):
 171:         """Reset all truncation tracking"""
 172:         self.events.clear()
 173:         for cat_list in self.per_category.values():
 174:             cat_list.clear()
 175:         self.session_total_truncations = 0
 176:         self.session_total_messages_removed = 0
 177:         self.session_total_tokens_freed = 0
 178: 
 179: 
 180: class ContextWindowManager:
 181:     """Manages token budgeting and content trimming for conversation context"""
 182: 
 183:     def __init__(
 184:         self,
 185:         model_config=None,
 186:         token_counter: Optional[Callable[[Any], int]] = None,
 187:         api_client=None,
 188:         config_obj: Optional[Any] = None,
 189:         max_context_images: Optional[int] = None,
 190:     ):
 191:         """
 192:         Initialize the context window manager.
 193: 
 194:         Args:
 195:             model_config: Optional model configuration with token limits
 196:             token_counter: Function to count tokens for content
 197:             api_client: API client for token counting
 198:             max_context_images: Maximum images to retain before trimming oldest
 199:         """
 200:         # Initialize truncation tracker
 201:         self.truncation_tracker = TruncationTracker()
 202: 
 203:         # Max images to keep in context (trim oldest when exceeded)
 204:         from penguin.constants import DEFAULT_MAX_CONTEXT_IMAGES
 205: 
 206:         self.max_context_images = (
 207:             max_context_images
 208:             if max_context_images is not None
 209:             else DEFAULT_MAX_CONTEXT_IMAGES
 210:         )
 211: 
 212:         # Context window budget - get from config.yml model.context_window
 213:         # NOTE: context_window = INPUT capacity (history), max_output_tokens = OUTPUT limit (generation)
 214:         self.max_context_window_tokens = None
 215: 
 216:         try:
 217:             from penguin.config import config
 218:             from penguin.llm.model_config import safe_context_window
 219: 
 220:             if "model" in config and isinstance(config["model"], dict):
 221: ... (truncated, showing first 220 lines of 1373)

   1: import asyncio
   2: from typing import Any, List, Optional, Tuple
   3: 
   4: from openai.types.beta.function_tool import FunctionTool as OpenAITool
   5: 
   6: from letta.log import get_logger
   7: from letta.schemas.agent import AgentState
   8: from letta.schemas.enums import MessageRole
   9: from letta.schemas.letta_message_content import TextContent
  10: from letta.schemas.memory import ContextWindowOverview
  11: from letta.schemas.message import Message
  12: from letta.schemas.user import User as PydanticUser
  13: from letta.services.context_window_calculator.token_counter import TokenCounter
  14: from letta.services.message_manager import MessageManager
  15: 
  16: logger = get_logger(__name__)
  17: 
  18: 
  19: class ContextWindowCalculator:
  20:     """Handles context window calculations with different token counting strategies"""
  21: 
  22:     @staticmethod
  23:     def extract_system_components(system_message: str) -> Tuple[str, str, str]:
  24:         """
  25:         Extract structured components from a formatted system message.
  26: 
  27:         Parses the system message to extract three distinct sections marked by XML-style tags:
  28:         - base_instructions: The core system prompt and agent instructions
  29:         - memory_blocks: The agent's core memory (persistent context)
  30:         - memory_metadata: Metadata about external memory systems
  31: 
  32:         Args:
  33:             system_message: A formatted system message containing XML-style section markers
  34: 
  35:         Returns:
  36:             A tuple of (system_prompt, core_memory, external_memory_summary)
  37:             Each component will be an empty string if its section is not found
  38: 
  39:         Note:
  40:             This method assumes a specific format with sections delimited by:
  41:             <base_instructions>, <memory_blocks>, and <memory_metadata> tags.
  42:             The extraction is position-based and expects sections in this order.
  43:         """
  44:         base_start = system_message.find("<base_instructions>")
  45:         memory_blocks_start = system_message.find("<memory_blocks>")
  46:         metadata_start = system_message.find("<memory_metadata>")
  47: 
  48:         system_prompt = ""
  49:         core_memory = ""
  50:         external_memory_summary = ""
  51: 
  52:         if base_start != -1 and memory_blocks_start != -1:
  53:             system_prompt = system_message[base_start:memory_blocks_start].strip()
  54: 
  55:         if memory_blocks_start != -1 and metadata_start != -1:
  56:             core_memory = system_message[memory_blocks_start:metadata_start].strip()
  57: 
  58:         if metadata_start != -1:
  59:             external_memory_summary = system_message[metadata_start:].strip()
  60: 
  61:         return system_prompt, core_memory, external_memory_summary
  62: 
  63:     @staticmethod
  64:     def extract_summary_memory(messages: List[Any]) -> Tuple[Optional[str], int]:
  65:         """
  66:         Extract summary memory from the message list if present.
  67: 
  68:         Summary memory is a special message injected at position 1 (after system message)
  69:         that contains a condensed summary of previous conversation history. This is used
  70:         when the full conversation history doesn't fit in the context window.
  71: 
  72:         Args:
  73:             messages: List of message objects to search for summary memory
  74: 
  75:         Returns:
  76:             A tuple of (summary_text, start_index) where:
  77:             - summary_text: The extracted summary content, or None if not found
  78:             - start_index: Index where actual conversation messages begin (1 or 2)
  79: 
  80:         Detection Logic:
  81:             Looks for a user message at index 1 containing the phrase
  82:             "The following is a summary of the previous" which indicates
  83:             it's a summarized conversation history rather than a real user message.
  84:         """
  85:         if (
  86:             len(messages) > 1
  87:             and messages[1].role == MessageRole.user
  88:             and messages[1].content
  89:             and len(messages[1].content) == 1
  90:             and isinstance(messages[1].content[0], TextContent)
  91:             and "The following is a summary of the previous " in messages[1].content[0].text
  92:         ):
  93:             summary_memory = messages[1].content[0].text
  94:             start_index = 2
  95:             return summary_memory, start_index
  96: 
  97:         return None, 1
  98: 
  99:     async def calculate_context_window(
 100:         self,
 101:         agent_state: AgentState,
 102:         actor: PydanticUser,
 103:         token_counter: TokenCounter,
 104:         message_manager: MessageManager,
 105:         system_message_compiled: Message,
 106:         num_archival_memories: int,
 107:         num_messages: int,
 108:     ) -> ContextWindowOverview:
 109:         """Calculate context window information using the provided token counter"""
 110:         messages = await message_manager.get_messages_by_ids_async(message_ids=agent_state.message_ids[1:], actor=actor)
 111:         in_context_messages = [system_message_compiled] + messages
 112: 
 113:         # Filter out None messages (can occur when system message is missing)
 114:         original_count = len(in_context_messages)
 115:         in_context_messages = [m for m in in_context_messages if m is not None]
 116:         if len(in_context_messages) < original_count:
 117:             logger.warning(
 118:                 f"Filtered out {original_count - len(in_context_messages)} None messages for agent {agent_state.id}. "
 119:                 f"This typically indicates missing system message or corrupted message data."
 120:             )
 121: 
 122:         # Convert messages to appropriate format
 123:         converted_messages = token_counter.convert_messages(in_context_messages)
 124: 
 125:         # Extract system components
 126:         system_prompt = ""
 127:         core_memory = ""
 128:         external_memory_summary = ""
 129: 
 130:         if (
 131:             in_context_messages
 132:             and in_context_messages[0].role == MessageRole.system
 133:             and in_context_messages[0].content
 134:             and len(in_context_messages[0].content) == 1
 135:             and isinstance(in_context_messages[0].content[0], TextContent)
 136:         ):
 137:             system_message = in_context_messages[0].content[0].text
 138:             system_prompt, core_memory, external_memory_summary = self.extract_system_components(system_message)
 139: 
 140:         # System prompt
 141:         system_prompt = system_prompt or agent_state.system
 142: 
 143:         # Extract summary memory
 144:         summary_memory, message_start_index = self.extract_summary_memory(in_context_messages)
 145: 
 146:         # Prepare tool definitions
 147:         available_functions_definitions = []
 148:         if agent_state.tools:
 149:             available_functions_definitions = [OpenAITool(type="function", function=f.json_schema) for f in agent_state.tools]
 150: 
 151:         # Count tokens concurrently
 152:         token_counts = await asyncio.gather(
 153:             token_counter.count_text_tokens(system_prompt),
 154:             token_counter.count_text_tokens(core_memory),
 155:             token_counter.count_text_tokens(external_memory_summary),
 156:             token_counter.count_text_tokens(summary_memory) if summary_memory else asyncio.sleep(0, result=0),
 157:             (
 158:                 token_counter.count_message_tokens(converted_messages[message_start_index:])
 159:                 if len(converted_messages) > message_start_index
 160:                 else asyncio.sleep(0, result=0)
 161:             ),
 162:             (
 163:                 token_counter.count_tool_tokens(available_functions_definitions)
 164:                 if available_functions_definitions
 165:                 else asyncio.sleep(0, result=0)
 166:             ),
 167:         )
 168: 
 169:         (
 170:             num_tokens_system,
 171:             num_tokens_core_memory,
 172:             num_tokens_external_memory_summary,
 173:             num_tokens_summary_memory,
 174:             num_tokens_messages,
 175:             num_tokens_available_functions_definitions,
 176:         ) = token_counts
 177: 
 178:         num_tokens_used_total = sum(token_counts)
 179: 
 180:         return ContextWindowOverview(
 181:             # context window breakdown (in messages)
 182:             num_messages=len(in_context_messages),
 183:             num_archival_memory=num_archival_memories,
 184:             num_recall_memory=num_messages,
 185:             num_tokens_external_memory_summary=num_tokens_external_memory_summary,
 186:             external_memory_summary=external_memory_summary,
 187:             # top-level information
 188:             context_window_size_max=agent_state.llm_config.context_window,
 189:             context_window_size_current=num_tokens_used_total,
 190:             # context window breakdown (in tokens)
 191:             num_tokens_system=num_tokens_system,
 192:             system_prompt=system_prompt,
 193:             num_tokens_core_memory=num_tokens_core_memory,
 194:             core_memory=core_memory,
 195:             num_tokens_summary_memory=num_tokens_summary_memory,
 196:             summary_memory=summary_memory,
 197:             num_tokens_messages=num_tokens_messages,
 198:             messages=in_context_messages,
 199:             # related to functions
 200:             num_tokens_functions_definitions=num_tokens_available_functions_definitions,
 201:             functions_definitions=available_functions_definitions,
 202:         )

   1: import asyncio
   2: import json
   3: import traceback
   4: from typing import List, Optional, Tuple, Union
   5: 
   6: from letta.agents.ephemeral_summary_agent import EphemeralSummaryAgent
   7: from letta.constants import (
   8:     DEFAULT_MESSAGE_TOOL,
   9:     DEFAULT_MESSAGE_TOOL_KWARG,
  10:     MESSAGE_SUMMARY_REQUEST_ACK,
  11:     TOOL_RETURN_TRUNCATION_CHARS,
  12: )
  13: from letta.errors import ContextWindowExceededError
  14: from letta.helpers.message_helper import convert_message_creates_to_messages
  15: from letta.llm_api.llm_client import LLMClient
  16: from letta.log import get_logger
  17: from letta.otel.tracing import trace_method
  18: from letta.prompts import gpt_summarize
  19: from letta.schemas.enums import AgentType, MessageRole, ProviderType
  20: from letta.schemas.letta_message_content import TextContent
  21: from letta.schemas.llm_config import LLMConfig
  22: from letta.schemas.message import Message, MessageCreate
  23: from letta.schemas.user import User
  24: from letta.services.agent_manager import AgentManager
  25: from letta.services.message_manager import MessageManager
  26: from letta.services.summarizer.enums import SummarizationMode
  27: from letta.system import package_summarize_message_no_counts
  28: from letta.utils import safe_create_task
  29: 
  30: logger = get_logger(__name__)
  31: 
  32: 
  33: # NOTE: legacy, new version is functional
  34: class Summarizer:
  35:     """
  36:     Handles summarization or trimming of conversation messages based on
  37:     the specified SummarizationMode. For now, we demonstrate a simple
  38:     static buffer approach but leave room for more advanced strategies.
  39:     """
  40: 
  41:     def __init__(
  42:         self,
  43:         mode: SummarizationMode,
  44:         summarizer_agent: Optional[Union[EphemeralSummaryAgent, "VoiceSleeptimeAgent"]] = None,
  45:         message_buffer_limit: int = 10,
  46:         message_buffer_min: int = 3,
  47:         partial_evict_summarizer_percentage: float = 0.30,
  48:         agent_manager: Optional[AgentManager] = None,
  49:         message_manager: Optional[MessageManager] = None,
  50:         actor: Optional[User] = None,
  51:         agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
  52:     ):
  53:         self.mode = mode
  54: 
  55:         # Need to do validation on this
  56:         # TODO: Move this to config
  57:         self.message_buffer_limit = message_buffer_limit
  58:         self.message_buffer_min = message_buffer_min
  59:         self.summarizer_agent = summarizer_agent
  60:         self.partial_evict_summarizer_percentage = partial_evict_summarizer_percentage
  61: 
  62:         # for partial buffer only
  63:         self.agent_manager = agent_manager
  64:         self.message_manager = message_manager
  65:         self.actor = actor
  66:         self.agent_id = agent_id
  67: 
  68:     @trace_method
  69:     async def summarize(
  70:         self,
  71:         in_context_messages: List[Message],
  72:         new_letta_messages: List[Message],
  73:         force: bool = False,
  74:         clear: bool = False,
  75:     ) -> Tuple[List[Message], bool]:
  76:         """
  77:         Summarizes or trims in_context_messages according to the chosen mode,
  78:         and returns the updated messages plus any optional "summary message".
  79: 
  80:         Args:
  81:             in_context_messages: The existing messages in the conversation's context.
  82:             new_letta_messages: The newly added Letta messages (just appended).
  83:             force: Force summarize even if the criteria is not met
  84: 
  85:         Returns:
  86:             (updated_messages, summary_message)
  87:             updated_messages: The new context after trimming/summary
  88:             summary_message: Optional summarization message that was created
  89:                              (could be appended to the conversation if desired)
  90:         """
  91:         if self.mode == SummarizationMode.STATIC_MESSAGE_BUFFER:
  92:             return self._static_buffer_summarization(
  93:                 in_context_messages,
  94:                 new_letta_messages,
  95:                 force=force,
  96:                 clear=clear,
  97:             )
  98:         elif self.mode == SummarizationMode.PARTIAL_EVICT_MESSAGE_BUFFER:
  99:             return await self._partial_evict_buffer_summarization(
 100:                 in_context_messages,
 101:                 new_letta_messages,
 102:                 force=force,
 103:                 clear=clear,
 104:             )
 105:         else:
 106:             # Fallback or future logic
 107:             return in_context_messages, False
 108: 
 109:     def fire_and_forget(self, coro):
 110:         task = safe_create_task(coro, label="summarizer_background_task")
 111: 
 112:         def callback(t):
 113:             try:
 114:                 t.result()  # This re-raises exceptions from the task
 115:             except Exception:
 116:                 logger.exception("Background task failed")
 117: 
 118:         task.add_done_callback(callback)
 119:         return task
 120: 
 121:     async def _partial_evict_buffer_summarization(
 122:         self,
 123:         in_context_messages: List[Message],
 124:         new_letta_messages: List[Message],
 125:         force: bool = False,
 126:         clear: bool = False,
 127:     ) -> Tuple[List[Message], bool]:
 128:         """Summarization as implemented in the original MemGPT loop, but using message count instead of token count.
 129:         Evict a partial amount of messages, and replace message[1] with a recursive summary.
 130: 
 131:         Note that this can't be made sync, because we're waiting on the summary to inject it into the context window,
 132:         unlike the version that writes it to a block.
 133: 
 134:         Unless force is True, don't summarize.
 135:         Ignore clear, we don't use it.
 136:         """
 137:         all_in_context_messages = in_context_messages + new_letta_messages
 138: 
 139:         if not force:
 140:             logger.debug("Not forcing summarization, returning in-context messages as is.")
 141:             return all_in_context_messages, False
 142: 
 143:         # First step: determine how many messages to retain
 144:         total_message_count = len(all_in_context_messages)
 145:         assert self.partial_evict_summarizer_percentage >= 0.0 and self.partial_evict_summarizer_percentage <= 1.0
 146:         target_message_start = round((1.0 - self.partial_evict_summarizer_percentage) * total_message_count)
 147:         logger.info(f"Target message count: {total_message_count}->{(total_message_count - target_message_start)}")
 148: 
 149:         # The summary message we'll insert is role 'user' (vs 'assistant', 'tool', or 'system')
 150:         # We are going to put it at index 1 (index 0 is the system message)
 151:         # That means that index 2 needs to be role 'assistant', so walk up the list starting at
 152:         # the target_message_count and find the first assistant message
 153:         for i in range(target_message_start, total_message_count):
 154:             if all_in_context_messages[i].role == MessageRole.assistant:
 155:                 assistant_message_index = i
 156:                 break
 157:         else:
 158:             raise ValueError(f"No assistant message found from indices {target_message_start} to {total_message_count}")
 159: 
 160:         # The sequence to summarize is index 1 -> assistant_message_index
 161:         messages_to_summarize = all_in_context_messages[1:assistant_message_index]
 162:         logger.info(f"Eviction indices: {1}->{assistant_message_index}(/{total_message_count})")
 163: 
 164:         # Dynamically get the LLMConfig from the summarizer agent
 165:         # Pretty cringe code here that we need the agent for this but we don't use it
 166:         agent_state = await self.agent_manager.get_agent_by_id_async(agent_id=self.agent_id, actor=self.actor)
 167: 
 168:         # TODO if we do this via the "agent", then we can more easily allow toggling on the memory block version
 169:         summary_message_str = await simple_summary(
 170:             messages=messages_to_summarize,
 171:             llm_config=agent_state.llm_config,
 172:             actor=self.actor,
 173:             include_ack=True,
 174:         )
 175: 
 176:         # TODO add counts back
 177:         # Recall message count
 178:         # num_recall_messages_current = await self.message_manager.size_async(actor=self.actor, agent_id=agent_state.id)
 179:         # num_messages_evicted = len(messages_to_summarize)
 180:         # num_recall_messages_hidden = num_recall_messages_total - len()
 181: 
 182:         # Create the summary message
 183:         summary_message_str_packed = package_summarize_message_no_counts(
 184:             summary=summary_message_str,
 185:             timezone=agent_state.timezone,
 186:         )
 187:         summary_message_obj = (
 188:             await convert_message_creates_to_messages(
 189:                 message_creates=[
 190:                     MessageCreate(
 191:                         role=MessageRole.user,
 192:                         content=[TextContent(text=summary_message_str_packed)],
 193:                     )
 194:                 ],
 195:                 agent_id=agent_state.id,
 196:                 timezone=agent_state.timezone,
 197:                 # We already packed, don't pack again
 198:                 wrap_user_message=False,
 199:                 wrap_system_message=False,
 200:                 run_id=None,  # TODO: add this
 201:             )
 202:         )[0]
 203: 
 204:         # Create the message in the DB
 205:         await self.message_manager.create_many_messages_async(
 206:             pydantic_msgs=[summary_message_obj],
 207:             actor=self.actor,
 208:             project_id=agent_state.project_id,
 209:             template_id=agent_state.template_id,
 210:         )
 211: 
 212:         updated_in_context_messages = all_in_context_messages[assistant_message_index:]
 213:         return [all_in_context_messages[0], summary_message_obj] + updated_in_context_messages, True
 214: 
 215:     def _static_buffer_summarization(
 216:         self,
 217:         in_context_messages: List[Message],
 218:         new_letta_messages: List[Message],
 219:         force: bool = False,
 220:         clear: bool = False,
 221:     ) -> Tuple[List[Message], bool]:
 222:         """
 223:         Implements static buffer summarization by maintaining a fixed-size message buffer (< N messages).
 224: 
 225:         Logic:
 226:         1. Combine existing context messages with new messages
 227:         2. If total messages <= buffer limit and not forced, return unchanged
 228:         3. Calculate how many messages to retain (0 if clear=True, otherwise message_buffer_min)
 229:         4. Find the trim index to keep the most recent messages while preserving user message boundaries
 230:         5. Evict older messages (everything between system message and trim index)
 231:         6. If summarizer agent is available, trigger background summarization of evicted messages
 232:         7. Return updated context with system message + retained recent messages
 233: 
 234:         Args:
 235:             in_context_messages: Existing conversation context messages
 236:             new_letta_messages: Newly added messages to append
 237:             force: Force summarization even if buffer limit not exceeded
 238:             clear: Clear all messages except system message (retain_count = 0)
 239: 
 240:         Returns:
 241:             Tuple of (updated_messages, was_summarized)
 242:             - updated_messages: New context after trimming/summarization
 243:             - was_summarized: True if messages were evicted and summarization triggered
 244:         """
 245: 
 246:         all_in_context_messages = in_context_messages + new_letta_messages
 247: 
 248:         if len(all_in_context_messages) <= self.message_buffer_limit and not force:
 249:             logger.info(
 250:                 f"Nothing to evict, returning in context messages as is. Current buffer length is {len(all_in_context_messages)}, limit is {self.message_buffer_limit}."
 251:             )
 252:             return all_in_context_messages, False
 253: 
 254:         retain_count = 0 if clear else self.message_buffer_min
 255: 
 256:         if not force:
 257:             logger.info(f"Buffer length hit {self.message_buffer_limit}, evicting until we retain only {retain_count} messages.")
 258:         else:
 259:             logger.info(f"Requested force summarization, evicting until we retain only {retain_count} messages.")
 260: 
 261: ... (truncated, showing first 260 lines of 657)

**Input:**

**Output:**
```
   1: from typing import List, Tuple
   2: 
   3: from letta.helpers.message_helper import convert_message_creates_to_messages
   4: from letta.log import get_logger
   5: from letta.otel.tracing import trace_method
   6: from letta.schemas.agent import AgentState
   7: from letta.schemas.enums import MessageRole
   8: from letta.schemas.letta_message_content import TextContent
   9: from letta.schemas.llm_config import LLMConfig
  10: from letta.schemas.message import Message, MessageCreate
  11: from letta.schemas.user import User
  12: from letta.services.context_window_calculator.token_counter import create_token_counter
  13: from letta.services.message_manager import MessageManager
  14: from letta.services.summarizer.summarizer import simple_summary
  15: from letta.services.summarizer.summarizer_config import CompactionSettings
  16: from letta.system import package_summarize_message_no_counts
  17: 
  18: logger = get_logger(__name__)
  19: 
  20: 
  21: # Safety margin for approximate token counting.
  22: # The bytes/4 heuristic underestimates by ~25-35% for JSON-serialized messages
  23: # due to structural overhead (brackets, quotes, colons) each becoming tokens.
  24: APPROX_TOKEN_SAFETY_MARGIN = 1.3
  25: 
  26: 
  27: async def count_tokens(actor: User, llm_config: LLMConfig, messages: List[Message]) -> int:
  28:     """Count tokens in messages using the appropriate token counter for the model configuration."""
  29:     token_counter = create_token_counter(
  30:         model_endpoint_type=llm_config.model_endpoint_type,
  31:         model=llm_config.model,
  32:         actor=actor,
  33:     )
  34:     converted_messages = token_counter.convert_messages(messages)
  35:     tokens = await token_counter.count_message_tokens(converted_messages)
  36: 
  37:     # Apply safety margin for approximate counting to avoid underestimating
  38:     from letta.services.context_window_calculator.token_counter import ApproxTokenCounter
  39: 
  40:     if isinstance(token_counter, ApproxTokenCounter):
  41:         return int(tokens * APPROX_TOKEN_SAFETY_MARGIN)
  42:     return tokens
  43: 
  44: 
  45: @trace_method
  46: async def summarize_via_sliding_window(
  47:     # Required to tag LLM calls
  48:     actor: User,
  49:     # Actual summarization configuration
  50:     llm_config: LLMConfig,
  51:     summarizer_config: CompactionSettings,
  52:     in_context_messages: List[Message],
  53:     # new_messages: List[Message],
  54: ) -> Tuple[str, List[Message]]:
  55:     """
  56:     If the total tokens is greater than the context window limit (or force=True),
  57:     then summarize and rearrange the in-context messages (with the summary in front).
  58: 
  59:     Finding the summarization cutoff point (target of final post-summarize count is N% of configured context window):
  60:     1. Start at a message index cutoff (1-N%)
  61:     2. Count tokens with system prompt, prior summary (if it exists), and messages past cutoff point (messages[0] + messages[cutoff:])
  62:     3. Is count(post_sum_messages) <= N% of configured context window?
  63:       3a. Yes -> create new summary with [prior summary, cutoff:], and safety truncate summary with char count
  64:       3b. No -> increment cutoff by 10%, and repeat
  65: 
  66:     Returns:
  67:     - The summary string
  68:     - The list of message IDs to keep in-context
  69:     """
  70:     system_prompt = in_context_messages[0]
  71:     total_message_count = len(in_context_messages)
  72: 
  73:     # cannot evict a pending approval request (will cause client-side errors)
  74:     if in_context_messages[-1].role == MessageRole.approval:
  75:         maximum_message_index = total_message_count - 2
  76:     else:
  77:         maximum_message_index = total_message_count - 1
  78: 
  79:     # Starts at N% (eg 70%), and increments up until 100%
  80:     message_count_cutoff_percent = max(
  81:         1 - summarizer_config.sliding_window_percentage, 0.10
  82:     )  # Some arbitrary minimum value (10%) to avoid negatives from badly configured summarizer percentage
  83:     eviction_percentage = summarizer_config.sliding_window_percentage
  84:     assert summarizer_config.sliding_window_percentage <= 1.0, "Sliding window percentage must be less than or equal to 1.0"
  85:     assistant_message_index = None
  86:     approx_token_count = llm_config.context_window
  87:     # valid_cutoff_roles = {MessageRole.assistant, MessageRole.approval}
  88:     valid_cutoff_roles = {MessageRole.assistant}
  89: 
  90:     # simple version: summarize(in_context[1:round(summarizer_config.sliding_window_percentage * len(in_context_messages))])
  91:     # this evicts 30% of the messages (via summarization) and keeps the remaining 70%
  92:     # problem: we need the cutoff point to be an assistant message, so will grow the cutoff point until we find an assistant message
  93:     # also need to grow the cutoff point until the token count is less than the target token count
  94: 
  95:     while approx_token_count >= (1 - summarizer_config.sliding_window_percentage) * llm_config.context_window and eviction_percentage < 1.0:
  96:         # more eviction percentage
  97:         eviction_percentage += 0.10
  98: 
  99:         # calculate message_cutoff_index
 100:         message_cutoff_index = round(eviction_percentage * total_message_count)
 101: 
 102:         # get index of first assistant message after the cutoff point ()
 103:         assistant_message_index = next(
 104:             (
 105:                 i
 106:                 for i in reversed(range(1, message_cutoff_index + 1))
 107:                 if i < len(in_context_messages) and in_context_messages[i].role in valid_cutoff_roles
 108:             ),
 109:             None,
 110:         )
 111:         if assistant_message_index is None:
 112:             logger.warning(f"No assistant message found for evicting up to index {message_cutoff_index}, incrementing eviction percentage")
 113:             continue
 114: 
 115:         # update token count
 116:         logger.info(f"Attempting to compact messages index 1:{assistant_message_index} messages")
 117:         post_summarization_buffer = [system_prompt] + in_context_messages[assistant_message_index:]
 118:         approx_token_count = await count_tokens(actor, llm_config, post_summarization_buffer)
 119:         logger.info(
 120:             f"Compacting messages index 1:{assistant_message_index} messages resulted in {approx_token_count} tokens, goal is {(1 - summarizer_config.sliding_window_percentage) * llm_config.context_window}"
 121:         )
 122: 
 123:     if assistant_message_index is None or eviction_percentage >= 1.0:
 124:         raise ValueError("No assistant message found for sliding window summarization")  # fall back to complete summarization
 125: 
 126:     if assistant_message_index >= maximum_message_index:
 127:         # need to keep the last message (might contain an approval request)
 128:         raise ValueError(f"Assistant message index {assistant_message_index} is at the end of the message buffer, skipping summarization")
 129: 
 130:     messages_to_summarize = in_context_messages[1:assistant_message_index]
 131:     logger.info(
 132:         f"Summarizing {len(messages_to_summarize)} messages, from index 1 to {assistant_message_index} (out of {total_message_count})"
 133:     )
 134: 
 135:     summary_message_str = await simple_summary(
 136:         messages=messages_to_summarize,
 137:         llm_config=llm_config,
 138:         actor=actor,
 139:         include_ack=bool(summarizer_config.prompt_acknowledgement),
 140:         prompt=summarizer_config.prompt,
 141:     )
 142: 
 143:     if summarizer_config.clip_chars is not None and len(summary_message_str) > summarizer_config.clip_chars:
 144:         logger.warning(f"Summary length {len(summary_message_str)} exceeds clip length {summarizer_config.clip_chars}. Truncating.")
 145:         summary_message_str = summary_message_str[: summarizer_config.clip_chars] + "... [summary truncated to fit]"
 146: 
 147:     updated_in_context_messages = in_context_messages[assistant_message_index:]
 148:     return summary_message_str, [system_prompt] + updated_in_context_messages
```
```

  18: export namespace SessionCompaction {
  19:   const log = Log.create({ service: "session.compaction" })
  20: 
  21:   export const Event = {
  22:     Compacted: BusEvent.define(

  25:         sessionID: z.string(),

  30:   export async function isOverflow(input: { tokens: MessageV2.Assistant["tokens"]; model: Provider.Model }) {
  31:     const config = await Config.get()
  32:     if (config.compaction?.auto === false) return false
  33:     const context = input.model.limit.context
  34:     if (context === 0) return false
  35:     const count = input.tokens.input + input.tokens.cache.read + input.tokens.output
  36:     const output = Math.min(input.model.limit.output, SessionPrompt.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX) || SessionPrompt.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX
  37:     const usable = input.model.limit.input || context - output
  38:     return count > usable
  39:   }
  40: 
  41:   export const PRUNE_MINIMUM = 20_000
  42:   export const PRUNE_PROTECT = 40_000
  43: 
  44:   const PRUNE_PROTECTED_TOOLS = ["skill"]
  45: 
  46:   // goes backwards through parts until there are 40_000 tokens worth of tool
  47:   // calls. then erases output of previous tool calls. idea is to throw away old
  48:   // tool calls that are no longer relevant.
  49:   export async function prune(input: { sessionID: string }) {
  50:     const config = await Config.get()
  51:     if (config.compaction?.prune === false) return
  52:     log.info("pruning")
  53:     const msgs = await Session.messages({ sessionID: input.sessionID })
  54:     let total = 0
  55:     let pruned = 0
  56:     const toPrune = []
  57:     let turns = 0
  58: 
  59:     loop: for (let msgIndex = msgs.length - 1; msgIndex >= 0; msgIndex--) {
  60:       const msg = msgs[msgIndex]
  61:       if (msg.info.role === "user") turns++
  62:       if (turns < 2) continue
  63:       if (msg.info.role === "assistant" && msg.info.summary) break loop
  64:       for (let partIndex = msg.parts.length - 1; partIndex >= 0; partIndex--) {
  65:         const part = msg.parts[partIndex]
  66:         if (part.type === "tool")
  67:           if (part.state.status === "completed") {
  68:             if (PRUNE_PROTECTED_TOOLS.includes(part.tool)) continue
  69: 
  70:             if (part.state.time.compacted) break loop
  71:             const estimate = Token.estimate(part.state.output)
  72:             total += estimate
  73:             if (total > PRUNE_PROTECT) {
  74:               pruned += estimate
  75:               toPrune.push(part)
  76:             }
  77:           }
  78:       }
  79:     }
  80:     log.info("found", { pruned, total })
  81:     if (pruned > PRUNE_MINIMUM) {
  82:       for (const part of toPrune) {
  83:         if (part.state.status === "completed") {
  84:           part.state.time.compacted = Date.now()
  85:           await Session.updatePart(part)
  86:         }
  87:       }
  88:       log.info("pruned", { count: toPrune.length })
  89:     }
  90:   }
  91: 
  92:   export async function process(input: {
  93:     parentID: string
  94:     messages: MessageV2.WithParts[]
  95:     sessionID: string
  96:     abort: AbortSignal
  97:     auto: boolean
  98:   }) {
  99:     const userMessage = input.messages.findLast((m) => m.info.id === input.parentID)!.info as MessageV2.User
 100:     const agent = await Agent.get("compaction")
 101:     const model = agent.model
 102:       ? await Provider.getModel(agent.model.providerID, agent.model.modelID)
 103:       : await Provider.getModel(userMessage.model.providerID, userMessage.model.modelID)
 104:     const msg = (await Session.updateMessage({
 105:       id: Identifier.ascending("message"),
 106:       role: "assistant",
 107:       parentID: input.parentID,
 108:       sessionID: input.sessionID,
 109:       mode: "compaction",
 110:       agent: "compaction",
 111:       summary: true,
 112:       path: {
 113:         cwd: Instance.directory,
 114:         root: Instance.worktree,
 115:       },
 116:       cost: 0,
 117:       tokens: {
 118:         output: 0,
 119:         input: 0,
 120:         reasoning: 0,
 121:         cache: { read: 0, write: 0 },
 122:       },
 123:       modelID: model.id,
 124:       providerID: model.providerID,
 125:       time: {
 126:         created: Date.now(),
 127:       },
 128:     })) as MessageV2.Assistant
 129:     const processor = SessionProcessor.create({
 130:       assistantMessage: msg,
 131:       sessionID: input.sessionID,
 132:       model,
 133:       abort: input.abort,
 134:     })
 135:     // Allow plugins to inject context or replace compaction prompt
 136:     const compacting = await Plugin.trigger(
 137:       "experimental.session.compacting",
 138:       { sessionID: input.sessionID },
 139:       { context: [], prompt: undefined },
 140:     )
 141:     const defaultPrompt =
 142:       "Provide a detailed prompt for continuing our conversation above. Focus on information that would be helpful for continuing the conversation, including what we did, what we're doing, which files we're working on, and what we're going to do next considering new session will not have access to our conversation."
 143:     const promptText = compacting.prompt ?? [defaultPrompt, ...compacting.context].join("\n\n")
 144:     const result = await processor.process({
 145:       user: userMessage,
 146:       agent,
 147:       abort: input.abort,
 148:       sessionID: input.sessionID,
 149:       tools: {},
 150:       system: [],
 151:       messages: [
 152:         ...MessageV2.toModelMessages(input.messages, model),
 153:         {
 154:           role: "user",
 155:           content: [
 156:             {
 157:               type: "text",
 158:               text: promptText,
 159:             },
 160:           ],
 161:         },
 162:       ],
 163:       model,
 164:     })
 165: 
 166:     if (result === "continue" && input.auto) {
 167:       const continueMsg = await Session.updateMessage({
 168:         id: Identifier.ascending("message"),
 169:         role: "user",
 170:         sessionID: input.sessionID,
 171:         time: {
 172:           created: Date.now(),
 173:         },
 174:         agent: userMessage.agent,
 175:         model: userMessage.model,
 176:       })
 177:       await Session.updatePart({
 178:         id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
 179:         messageID: continueMsg.id,
 180:         sessionID: input.sessionID,
 181:         type: "text",
 182:         synthetic: true,
 183:         text: "Continue if you have next steps",
 184:         time: {
 185:           start: Date.now(),
 186:           end: Date.now(),
 187:         },
 188:       })
 189:     }
 190:     if (processor.message.error) return "stop"
 191:     Bus.publish(Event.Compacted, { sessionID: input.sessionID })
 192:     return "continue"
 193:   }
 194:

   1: import { MessageV2 } from "./message-v2"
   2: import { Log } from "@/util/log"
   3: import { Identifier } from "@/id/id"
   4: import { Session } from "."
   5: import { Agent } from "@/agent/agent"
   6: import { Snapshot } from "@/snapshot"
   7: import { SessionSummary } from "./summary"
   8: import { Bus } from "@/bus"
   9: import { SessionRetry } from "./retry"
  10: import { SessionStatus } from "./status"
  11: import { Plugin } from "@/plugin"
  12: import type { Provider } from "@/provider/provider"
  13: import { LLM } from "./llm"
  14: import { Config } from "@/config/config"
  15: import { SessionCompaction } from "./compaction"
  16: import { PermissionNext } from "@/permission/next"
  17: import { Question } from "@/question"
  18: 
  19: export namespace SessionProcessor {
  20:   const DOOM_LOOP_THRESHOLD = 3
  21:   const log = Log.create({ service: "session.processor" })
  22: 
  23:   export type Info = Awaited<ReturnType<typeof create>>
  24:   export type Result = Awaited<ReturnType<Info["process"]>>
  25: 
  26:   export function create(input: {
  27:     assistantMessage: MessageV2.Assistant
  28:     sessionID: string
  29:     model: Provider.Model
  30:     abort: AbortSignal
  31:   }) {
  32:     const toolcalls: Record<string, MessageV2.ToolPart> = {}
  33:     let snapshot: string | undefined
  34:     let blocked = false
  35:     let attempt = 0
  36:     let needsCompaction = false
  37: 
  38:     const result = {
  39:       get message() {
  40:         return input.assistantMessage
  41:       },
  42:       partFromToolCall(toolCallID: string) {
  43:         return toolcalls[toolCallID]
  44:       },
  45:       async process(streamInput: LLM.StreamInput) {
  46:         log.info("process")
  47:         needsCompaction = false
  48:         const shouldBreak = (await Config.get()).experimental?.continue_loop_on_deny !== true
  49:         while (true) {
  50:           try {
  51:             let currentText: MessageV2.TextPart | undefined
  52:             let reasoningMap: Record<string, MessageV2.ReasoningPart> = {}
  53:             const stream = await LLM.stream(streamInput)
  54: 
  55:             for await (const value of stream.fullStream) {
  56:               input.abort.throwIfAborted()
  57:               switch (value.type) {
  58:                 case "start":
  59:                   SessionStatus.set(input.sessionID, { type: "busy" })
  60:                   break
  61: 
  62:                 case "reasoning-start":
  63:                   if (value.id in reasoningMap) {
  64:                     continue
  65:                   }
  66:                   reasoningMap[value.id] = {
  67:                     id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
  68:                     messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
  69:                     sessionID: input.assistantMessage.sessionID,
  70:                     type: "reasoning",
  71:                     text: "",
  72:                     time: {
  73:                       start: Date.now(),
  74:                     },
  75:                     metadata: value.providerMetadata,
  76:                   }
  77:                   break
  78: 
  79:                 case "reasoning-delta":
  80:                   if (value.id in reasoningMap) {
  81:                     const part = reasoningMap[value.id]
  82:                     part.text += value.text
  83:                     if (value.providerMetadata) part.metadata = value.providerMetadata
  84:                     if (part.text) await Session.updatePart({ part, delta: value.text })
  85:                   }
  86:                   break
  87: 
  88:                 case "reasoning-end":
  89:                   if (value.id in reasoningMap) {
  90:                     const part = reasoningMap[value.id]
  91:                     part.text = part.text.trimEnd()
  92: 
  93:                     part.time = {
  94:                       ...part.time,
  95:                       end: Date.now(),
  96:                     }
  97:                     if (value.providerMetadata) part.metadata = value.providerMetadata
  98:                     await Session.updatePart(part)
  99:                     delete reasoningMap[value.id]
 100:                   }
 101:                   break
 102: 
 103:                 case "tool-input-start":
 104:                   const part = await Session.updatePart({
 105:                     id: toolcalls[value.id]?.id ?? Identifier.ascending("part"),
 106:                     messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 107:                     sessionID: input.assistantMessage.sessionID,
 108:                     type: "tool",
 109:                     tool: value.toolName,
 110:                     callID: value.id,
 111:                     state: {
 112:                       status: "pending",
 113:                       input: {},
 114:                       raw: "",
 115:                     },
 116:                   })
 117:                   toolcalls[value.id] = part as MessageV2.ToolPart
 118:                   break
 119: 
 120:                 case "tool-input-delta":
 121:                   break
 122: 
 123:                 case "tool-input-end":
 124:                   break
 125: 
 126:                 case "tool-call": {
 127:                   const match = toolcalls[value.toolCallId]
 128:                   if (match) {
 129:                     const part = await Session.updatePart({
 130:                       ...match,
 131:                       tool: value.toolName,
 132:                       state: {
 133:                         status: "running",
 134:                         input: value.input,
 135:                         time: {
 136:                           start: Date.now(),
 137:                         },
 138:                       },
 139:                       metadata: value.providerMetadata,
 140:                     })
 141:                     toolcalls[value.toolCallId] = part as MessageV2.ToolPart
 142: 
 143:                     const parts = await MessageV2.parts(input.assistantMessage.id)
 144:                     const lastThree = parts.slice(-DOOM_LOOP_THRESHOLD)
 145: 
 146:                     if (
 147:                       lastThree.length === DOOM_LOOP_THRESHOLD &&
 148:                       lastThree.every(
 149:                         (p) =>
 150:                           p.type === "tool" &&
 151:                           p.tool === value.toolName &&
 152:                           p.state.status !== "pending" &&
 153:                           JSON.stringify(p.state.input) === JSON.stringify(value.input),
 154:                       )
 155:                     ) {
 156:                       const agent = await Agent.get(input.assistantMessage.agent)
 157:                       await PermissionNext.ask({
 158:                         permission: "doom_loop",
 159:                         patterns: [value.toolName],
 160:                         sessionID: input.assistantMessage.sessionID,
 161:                         metadata: {
 162:                           tool: value.toolName,
 163:                           input: value.input,
 164:                         },
 165:                         always: [value.toolName],
 166:                         ruleset: agent.permission,
 167:                       })
 168:                     }
 169:                   }
 170:                   break
 171:                 }
 172:                 case "tool-result": {
 173:                   const match = toolcalls[value.toolCallId]
 174:                   if (match && match.state.status === "running") {
 175:                     await Session.updatePart({
 176:                       ...match,
 177:                       state: {
 178:                         status: "completed",
 179:                         input: value.input ?? match.state.input,
 180:                         output: value.output.output,
 181:                         metadata: value.output.metadata,
 182:                         title: value.output.title,
 183:                         time: {
 184:                           start: match.state.time.start,
 185:                           end: Date.now(),
 186:                         },
 187:                         attachments: value.output.attachments,
 188:                       },
 189:                     })
 190: 
 191:                     delete toolcalls[value.toolCallId]
 192:                   }
 193:                   break
 194:                 }
 195: 
 196:                 case "tool-error": {
 197:                   const match = toolcalls[value.toolCallId]
 198:                   if (match && match.state.status === "running") {
 199:                     await Session.updatePart({
 200:                       ...match,
 201:                       state: {
 202:                         status: "error",
 203:                         input: value.input ?? match.state.input,
 204:                         error: (value.error as any).toString(),
 205:                         time: {
 206:                           start: match.state.time.start,
 207:                           end: Date.now(),
 208:                         },
 209:                       },
 210:                     })
 211: 
 212:                     if (
 213:                       value.error instanceof PermissionNext.RejectedError ||
 214:                       value.error instanceof Question.RejectedError
 215:                     ) {
 216:                       blocked = shouldBreak
 217:                     }
 218:                     delete toolcalls[value.toolCallId]
 219:                   }
 220:                   break
 221:                 }
 222:                 case "error":
 223:                   throw value.error
 224: 
 225:                 case "start-step":
 226:                   snapshot = await Snapshot.track()
 227:                   await Session.updatePart({
 228:                     id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
 229:                     messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 230:                     sessionID: input.sessionID,
 231:                     snapshot,
 232:                     type: "step-start",
 233:                   })
 234:                   break
 235: 
 236:                 case "finish-step":
 237:                   const usage = Session.getUsage({
 238:                     model: input.model,
 239:                     usage: value.usage,
 240:                     metadata: value.providerMetadata,
 241:                   })
 242:                   input.assistantMessage.finish = value.finishReason
 243:                   input.assistantMessage.cost += usage.cost
 244:                   input.assistantMessage.tokens = usage.tokens
 245:                   await Session.updatePart({
 246:                     id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
 247:                     reason: value.finishReason,
 248:                     snapshot: await Snapshot.track(),
 249:                     messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 250:                     sessionID: input.assistantMessage.sessionID,
 251:                     type: "step-finish",
 252:                     tokens: usage.tokens,
 253:                     cost: usage.cost,
 254:                   })
 255:                   await Session.updateMessage(input.assistantMessage)
 256:                   if (snapshot) {
 257:                     const patch = await Snapshot.patch(snapshot)
 258:                     if (patch.files.length) {
 259:                       await Session.updatePart({
 260:                         id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
 261:                         messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 262:                         sessionID: input.sessionID,
 263:                         type: "patch",
 264:                         hash: patch.hash,
 265:                         files: patch.files,
 266:                       })
 267:                     }
 268:                     snapshot = undefined
 269:                   }
 270:                   SessionSummary.summarize({
 271:                     sessionID: input.sessionID,
 272:                     messageID: input.assistantMessage.parentID,
 273:                   })
 274:                   if (await SessionCompaction.isOverflow({ tokens: usage.tokens, model: input.model })) {
 275:                     needsCompaction = true
 276:                   }
 277:                   break
 278: 
 279:                 case "text-start":
 280:                   currentText = {
 281:                     id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
 282:                     messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 283:                     sessionID: input.assistantMessage.sessionID,
 284:                     type: "text",
 285:                     text: "",
 286:                     time: {
 287:                       start: Date.now(),
 288:                     },
 289:                     metadata: value.providerMetadata,
 290:                   }
 291:                   break
 292: 
 293:                 case "text-delta":
 294:                   if (currentText) {
 295:                     currentText.text += value.text
 296:                     if (value.providerMetadata) currentText.metadata = value.providerMetadata
 297:                     if (currentText.text)
 298:                       await Session.updatePart({
 299:                         part: currentText,
 300:                         delta: value.text,
 301:                       })
 302:                   }
 303:                   break
 304: 
 305:                 case "text-end":
 306:                   if (currentText) {
 307:                     currentText.text = currentText.text.trimEnd()
 308:                     const textOutput = await Plugin.trigger(
 309:                       "experimental.text.complete",
 310:                       {
 311:                         sessionID: input.sessionID,
 312:                         messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 313:                         partID: currentText.id,
 314:                       },
 315:                       { text: currentText.text },
 316:                     )
 317:                     currentText.text = textOutput.text
 318:                     currentText.time = {
 319:                       start: Date.now(),
 320:                       end: Date.now(),
 321:                     }
 322:                     if (value.providerMetadata) currentText.metadata = value.providerMetadata
 323:                     await Session.updatePart(currentText)
 324:                   }
 325:                   currentText = undefined
 326:                   break
 327: 
 328:                 case "finish":
 329:                   break
 330: 
 331:                 default:
 332:                   log.info("unhandled", {
 333:                     ...value,
 334:                   })
 335:                   continue
 336:               }
 337:               if (needsCompaction) break
 338:             }
 339:           } catch (e: any) {
 340:             log.error("process", {
 341:               error: e,
 342:               stack: JSON.stringify(e.stack),
 343:             })
 344:             const error = MessageV2.fromError(e, { providerID: input.model.providerID })
 345:             const retry = SessionRetry.retryable(error)
 346:             if (retry !== undefined) {
 347:               attempt++
 348:               const delay = SessionRetry.delay(attempt, error.name === "APIError" ? error : undefined)
 349:               SessionStatus.set(input.sessionID, {
 350:                 type: "retry",
 351:                 attempt,
 352:                 message: retry,
 353:                 next: Date.now() + delay,
 354:               })
 355:               await SessionRetry.sleep(delay, input.abort).catch(() => {})
 356:               continue
 357:             }
 358:             input.assistantMessage.error = error
 359:             Bus.publish(Session.Event.Error, {
 360:               sessionID: input.assistantMessage.sessionID,
 361:               error: input.assistantMessage.error,
 362:             })
 363:           }
 364:           if (snapshot) {
 365:             const patch = await Snapshot.patch(snapshot)
 366:             if (patch.files.length) {
 367:               await Session.updatePart({
 368:                 id: Identifier.ascending("part"),
 369:                 messageID: input.assistantMessage.id,
 370:                 sessionID: input.sessionID,
 371:                 type: "patch",
 372:                 hash: patch.hash,
 373:                 files: patch.files,
 374:               })
 375:             }
 376:             snapshot = undefined
 377:           }
 378:           const p = await MessageV2.parts(input.assistantMessage.id)
 379:           for (const part of p) {
 380:             if (part.type === "tool" && part.state.status !== "completed" && part.state.status !== "error") {
 381:               await Session.updatePart({
 382:                 ...part,
 383:                 state: {
 384:                   ...part.state,
 385:                   status: "error",
 386:                   error: "Tool execution aborted",
 387:                   time: {
 388:                     start: Date.now(),
 389:                     end: Date.now(),
 390:                   },
 391:                 },
 392:               })
 393:             }
 394:           }
 395:           input.assistantMessage.time.completed = Date.now()
 396:           await Session.updateMessage(input.assistantMessage)
 397:           if (needsCompaction) return "compact"
 398:           if (blocked) return "stop"
 399:           if (input.assistantMessage.error) return "stop"
 400:           return "continue"
 401:         }
 402:       },
 403:     }
 404:     return result
 405:   }
 406: }
```

**Input:**

}
```
**Output:**
```
   1: import { Provider } from "@/provider/provider"
   2: 
   3: import { fn } from "@/util/fn"
   4: import z from "zod"
   5: import { Session } from "."
   6: 
   7: import { MessageV2 } from "./message-v2"
   8: import { Identifier } from "@/id/id"
   9: import { Snapshot } from "@/snapshot"
  10: 
  11: import { Log } from "@/util/log"
  12: import path from "path"
  13: import { Instance } from "@/project/instance"
  14: import { Storage } from "@/storage/storage"
  15: import { Bus } from "@/bus"
  16: 
  17: import { LLM } from "./llm"
  18: import { Agent } from "@/agent/agent"
  19: 
  20: export namespace SessionSummary {
  21:   const log = Log.create({ service: "session.summary" })
  22: 
  23:   function unquoteGitPath(input: string) {
  24:     if (!input.startsWith('"')) return input
  25:     if (!input.endsWith('"')) return input
  26:     const body = input.slice(1, -1)
  27:     const bytes: number[] = []
  28: 
  29:     for (let i = 0; i < body.length; i++) {
  30:       const char = body[i]!
  31:       if (char !== "\\") {
  32:         bytes.push(char.charCodeAt(0))
  33:         continue
  34:       }
  35: 
  36:       const next = body[i + 1]
  37:       if (!next) {
  38:         bytes.push("\\".charCodeAt(0))
  39:         continue
  40:       }
  41: 
  42:       if (next >= "0" && next <= "7") {
  43:         const chunk = body.slice(i + 1, i + 4)
  44:         const match = chunk.match(/^[0-7]{1,3}/)
  45:         if (!match) {
  46:           bytes.push(next.charCodeAt(0))
  47:           i++
  48:           continue
  49:         }
  50:         bytes.push(parseInt(match[0], 8))
  51:         i += match[0].length
  52:         continue
  53:       }
  54: 
  55:       const escaped =
  56:         next === "n"
  57:           ? "\n"
  58:           : next === "r"
  59:             ? "\r"
  60:             : next === "t"
  61:               ? "\t"
  62:               : next === "b"
  63:                 ? "\b"
  64:                 : next === "f"
  65:                   ? "\f"
  66:                   : next === "v"
  67:                     ? "\v"
  68:                     : next === "\\" || next === '"'
  69:                       ? next
  70:                       : undefined
  71: 
  72:       bytes.push((escaped ?? next).charCodeAt(0))
  73:       i++
  74:     }
  75: 
  76:     return Buffer.from(bytes).toString()
  77:   }
  78: 
  79:   export const summarize = fn(
  80:     z.object({
  81:       sessionID: z.string(),
  82:       messageID: z.string(),
  83:     }),
  84:     async (input) => {
  85:       const all = await Session.messages({ sessionID: input.sessionID })
  86:       await Promise.all([
  87:         summarizeSession({ sessionID: input.sessionID, messages: all }),
  88:         summarizeMessage({ messageID: input.messageID, messages: all }),
  89:       ])
  90:     },
  91:   )
  92: 
  93:   async function summarizeSession(input: { sessionID: string; messages: MessageV2.WithParts[] }) {
  94:     const files = new Set(
  95:       input.messages
  96:         .flatMap((x) => x.parts)
  97:         .filter((x) => x.type === "patch")
  98:         .flatMap((x) => x.files)
  99:         .map((x) => path.relative(Instance.worktree, x).replaceAll("\\", "/")),
 100:     )
 101:     const diffs = await computeDiff({ messages: input.messages }).then((x) =>
 102:       x.filter((x) => {
 103:         return files.has(x.file)
 104:       }),
 105:     )
 106:     await Session.update(input.sessionID, (draft) => {
 107:       draft.summary = {
 108:         additions: diffs.reduce((sum, x) => sum + x.additions, 0),
 109:         deletions: diffs.reduce((sum, x) => sum + x.deletions, 0),
 110:         files: diffs.length,
 111:       }
 112:     })
 113:     await Storage.write(["session_diff", input.sessionID], diffs)
 114:     Bus.publish(Session.Event.Diff, {
 115:       sessionID: input.sessionID,
 116:       diff: diffs,
 117:     })
 118:   }
 119: 
 120:   async function summarizeMessage(input: { messageID: string; messages: MessageV2.WithParts[] }) {
 121:     const messages = input.messages.filter(
 122:       (m) => m.info.id === input.messageID || (m.info.role === "assistant" && m.info.parentID === input.messageID),
 123:     )
 124:     const msgWithParts = messages.find((m) => m.info.id === input.messageID)!
 125:     const userMsg = msgWithParts.info as MessageV2.User
 126:     const diffs = await computeDiff({ messages })
 127:     userMsg.summary = {
 128:       ...userMsg.summary,
 129:       diffs,
 130:     }
 131:     await Session.updateMessage(userMsg)
 132: 
 133:     const textPart = msgWithParts.parts.find((p) => p.type === "text" && !p.synthetic) as MessageV2.TextPart
 134:     if (textPart && !userMsg.summary?.title) {
 135:       const agent = await Agent.get("title")
 136:       if (!agent) return
 137:       const stream = await LLM.stream({
 138:         agent,
 139:         user: userMsg,
 140:         tools: {},
 141:         model: agent.model
 142:           ? await Provider.getModel(agent.model.providerID, agent.model.modelID)
 143:           : ((await Provider.getSmallModel(userMsg.model.providerID)) ??
 144:             (await Provider.getModel(userMsg.model.providerID, userMsg.model.modelID))),
 145:         small: true,
 146:         messages: [
 147:           {
 148:             role: "user" as const,
 149:             content: `
 150:               The following is the text to summarize:
 151:               <text>
 152:               ${textPart?.text ?? ""}
 153:               </text>
 154:             `,
 155:           },
 156:         ],
 157:         abort: new AbortController().signal,
 158:         sessionID: userMsg.sessionID,
 159:         system: [],
 160:         retries: 3,
 161:       })
 162:       const result = await stream.text
 163:       log.info("title", { title: result })
 164:       userMsg.summary.title = result
 165:       await Session.updateMessage(userMsg)
 166:     }
 167:   }
 168: 
 169:   export const diff = fn(
 170:     z.object({
 171:       sessionID: Identifier.schema("session"),
 172:       messageID: Identifier.schema("message").optional(),
 173:     }),
 174:     async (input) => {
 175:       const diffs = await Storage.read<Snapshot.FileDiff[]>(["session_diff", input.sessionID]).catch(() => [])
 176:       const next = diffs.map((item) => {
 177:         const file = unquoteGitPath(item.file)
 178:         if (file === item.file) return item
 179:         return {
 180:           ...item,
 181:           file,
 182:         }
 183:       })
 184:       const changed = next.some((item, i) => item.file !== diffs[i]?.file)
 185:       if (changed) Storage.write(["session_diff", input.sessionID], next).catch(() => {})
 186:       return next
 187:     },
 188:   )
 189: 
 190:   export async function computeDiff(input: { messages: MessageV2.WithParts[] }) {
 191:     let from: string | undefined
 192:     let to: string | undefined
 193: 
 194:     // scan assistant messages to find earliest from and latest to
 195:     // snapshot
 196:     for (const item of input.messages) {
 197:       if (!from) {
 198:         for (const part of item.parts) {
 199:           if (part.type === "step-start" && part.snapshot) {
 200:             from = part.snapshot
 201:             break
 202:           }
 203:         }
 204:       }
 205: 
 206:       for (const part of item.parts) {
 207:         if (part.type === "step-finish" && part.snapshot) {
 208:           to = part.snapshot
 209:           break
 210:         }
 211:       }
 212:     }
 213: 
 214:     if (from && to) return Snapshot.diffFull(from, to)
 215:     return []
 216:   }
 217: }
